races.race_name: Weatherbys Global Stallions App Handicap

runners.name: Double Rush
runners.spotlight: Progressive sort for Charles Hills until disappointing in September on last appearance; commenced last season with two wins, including over C&D at this meeting, and warrants plenty of respect back here on debut for Andrew Balding with a resumption of improvement possible.
runners.comment: Mostly progressive for Charles Hills; won at this meeting last year; highly respected

runners.name: Divine Libra
runners.spotlight: Has course form and is capable of being involved; however, has gained both handicap wins at Chester, latest in May 2024, and may be more interesting back there with this reappearance under his belt.
runners.comment: Has gained both handicap wins at Chester; may be more interesting back there

runners.name: Coul Angel
runners.spotlight: Successful over C&D (off 15lb lower) seven months ago in last turf attempt and has subsequently developed into a useful AW handicapper; possibilities, provided he transfers the improvement back to this sphere.
runners.comment: Won over C&D in last turf attempt; useful AW handicapper since; possibilities

runners.name: Realign
runners.spotlight: Gelded prior to his autumn starts, winning at Haydock (6f) then perhaps hindered by heavy ground at Doncaster; lightly raced, in excellent hands and may still have further progress in him; remains of interest.
runners.comment: Lightly raced, in excellent hands and may still have further progress in him

runners.name: Humam
runners.spotlight: Gained his final win for previous yard and sole placed effort for current stable over 7f; beaten favourite in Italian Listed race over 6f when last seen; gelded since; may ideally need a step back up in trip.
runners.comment: Gelded since last run; gives impression he may ideally need a return to 7f

runners.name: Rousing Encore
runners.spotlight: Creditable second at Newcastle (6f, AW) on seasonal debut but looks no certainty to go one better in this stronger grade, especially having gained both wins last year off a 6lb lower mark.
runners.comment: Ran creditably on reappearance but this is a step back up in class

runners.name: Baldomero
runners.spotlight: Three wins during his latest spell on AW; close second to Dark Thirty in this contest two years ago but needs to rebound, having been comfortably held returned to turf 17 days ago.
runners.comment: Second in this race two years ago; comfortably held back on turf 17 days ago

runners.name: Eternal Sunshine
runners.spotlight: Won last year's Portland during a great spell of form that has come to an end with duck eggs in last two appearances; perhaps best watched until she shows clear signs of a revival.
runners.comment: Great spell of form has come to an end with duck eggs on last two outings

runners.name: Andesite
runners.spotlight: Finished last in final start for Karl Burke and midfield effort on reappearance (AW) took his record since debut success to 0-8; chance largely depends on how well he responds to first-time headgear.
runners.comment: Record of 0-8 since debut win; first-time headgear needs to help

runners.name: Eye Of Dubai
runners.spotlight: Capable of being involved but is by no means certain to shine in this spring reappearance, having shown his best handicap form in autumn months; the market signals may offer further guidance.
runners.comment: By no means certain to shine, having shown best handicap form in autumn months

runners.name: Diligently
runners.spotlight: Has gained both wins during the month of August but ran well (off current mark) on spring reappearance last term and when second to Eternal Sunshine on final outing; gelded since; each-way possibilities.
runners.comment: Both wins in August but ran well on spring reappearance last term

runners.name: Dark Thirty
runners.spotlight: Had two attempts at this race when with Richard Hannon, winning off 4lb higher in 2024 and losing his chance with a poor start last year; likely player if back to best in this second outing for new stable.
runners.comment: Landed this race off 4lb higher in 2024; second outing for new stable

runners.name: Invictus Gold
runners.spotlight: Ran respectably in AW Listed event when last seen; two attempts over C&D comprise a good third to Double Rush at this meeting 12 months ago and a ready success on 2,000 Guineas day (good); solid chance.
runners.comment: Two attempts over C&D comprise a good third to Double Rush then a ready success; solid

races.race_name: Betway Feilden Stakes (Listed Race)

runners.name: Isaac Newton
runners.spotlight: Ballydoyle colt who gained his maiden win at Glorious Goodwood then had a couple of French assignments, finishing fifth in a valuable conditions race at Longchamp (form has substance) and good fourth in the Criterium de Saint-Cloud (1m2f Group 1); leading player on the figures.
runners.comment: Ballydoyle colt; ran well in the Criterium de Saint-Cloud; leading player on the figures

runners.name: Bourbon Blues
runners.spotlight: Ended last season at the top of his game, going very close in a 1m2f nursery at Newmarket and a 1m1f Group 3 at Chantilly; has raced nine times already, however, and isn't sure to progress much further this term.
runners.comment: Useful sort but has raced nine times already and isn't sure to progress much further
runners.owner: M Hughes, M Kerr-Dineen And Partners

runners.name: Shayem
runners.spotlight: Beaten long way in Saudi Arabia in sole outing this year; 3-4 on home soil and made all for a clearcut success in Listed event at Pontefract (1m, soft) the final time; probably needs to resume that progress to defy a 3lb penalty in this rather deeper race for the grade.
runners.comment: Record of 3-4 on home soil features a Listed win; this is a deeper race for the grade

runners.name: Morshdi
runners.spotlight: Twice-raced colt who is brother to a Group 2 winner for his owner and seems likely to improve further; second at Sandown (7f) and went one better at York (1m, good) where he held on by a neck from a rival who subsequently ran well in Group 3; possibilities.
runners.comment: Twice-raced colt who is likely to build on his York win and improve further; possibilities

runners.name: Poseidon's Warrior
runners.spotlight: Sea The Stars colt who cost 900,000euros as a yearling; close second at Haydock (1m) and justified favouritism from the front at Goodwood (1m1f, good) where he drew clear to win by 5l; represents the powerful Appleby/Buick team and looks a useful prospect; respected.
runners.comment: Drew clear for 5l win at Goodwood and looks a useful prospect for his powerful stable

runners.name: Wareeth
runners.spotlight: Sea The Stars colt who cost 510,000gns as a yearling; had his rivals strung out like jumpers en route to an impressive performance at Newcastle (1m, AW) in March when he scored by 14l with a further 9l back to the third; interesting contender who brings major potential to this higher grade.
runners.comment: Had his rivals strung out like jumpers en route to an impressive performance at Newcastle

runners.name: Command The Stars
runners.spotlight: Record of 3-6 last term featured a Listed win (1m) on French soil, which followed a good effort in nursery at the York Ebor festival; failed to get home over 1m2f at Saint-Cloud final start; this looks a stiff task under 3lb penalty returned to domestic duty.
runners.comment: Record of 3-6 features a French Listed win; this looks a stiffer task under 3lb penalty
runners.owner: James Paul Services To Industry Ltd

races.race_name: Betway Earl Of Sefton Stakes (Group 3)

runners.name: Boiling Point
runners.spotlight: Can lead and goes well here, as he showed again when defying top-weight in the Cambridgeshire handicap over C&D (good to firm) in September; left the impression the run was just needed and/or the trip stretched him when close fourth of five in Winter Derby at Lingfield (1m2f, AW) in February; a bold bid is very much on the cards.
runners.comment: Won Cambridgeshire (C&D) last year; solid comeback in February and bold bid looks likely
runners.owner: Exors Of The Late Sheikh Mohammed Obaid

runners.name: Persica
runners.spotlight: Had Ryan Moore on board when winning this race (good to soft) last year and produced another big effort for that rider when landing similar event at Epsom (good) in June; didn't match that form subsequently but has been gelded and no surprise if he goes well in the bid to retain his crown under Oisin Murphy this time; entered in Group 1 Lockinge Stakes.
runners.comment: Won this last year; big effort to land similar event in June; gelded and needs respect

runners.name: Skukuza
runners.spotlight: Won a valuable Curragh handicap last May and impressed in a Listed win there (also 1m, good) the following month; again had Ryan Moore riding when he looked a shade unlucky in York Group 3 (1m1f, good to firm) in August; finished last year with good efforts in Group 2s at Leopardstown and Longchamp; back in that sort of form when close fifth in Saint-Cloud Group 3 (1m, soft) 17 days ago and he's a likely player with Moore retaking the reins.
runners.comment: Bit unlucky in 1m1f Group 3 in August; fine efforts in Gr2s latterly; fit and can go well

runners.name: Damysus
runners.spotlight: Third in good-ground Sandown Classic Trial and second in Group 2 Dante at York (good to firm) last spring; finished last in the Derby but firmly back on track when landing a 1m2f Listed race at Deauville (good to soft) in August and signed off with Group 3 C&D win in October; carries 3lb penalty but holds a Group 1 Lockinge Stakes entry and he's the sort to kick on again this year.
runners.comment: Banished memories of Derby flop with Listed and C&D Group 3 wins; key player on return

runners.name: King's Gambit
runners.spotlight: No wins in nine Group/Listed races since landing the London Gold Cup at Newbury on his seasonal/handicap debut in 2024; frequently ran well, though, albeit with this trip looking a bare minimum when fifth of eight in York Group 3 in August on his penultimate start; can't be confidently ruled out but will need to resume in top order to make his Group-race breakthrough.
runners.comment: 0-9 in Group/Listeds since notable handicap win in 2024; trip on the short side for return

runners.name: Prague
runners.spotlight: Announced himself when winning Group 2 here (1m, soft) in September 2024 but 0-8 since and he's become a little tricky to place; had wind surgery in December, which might help, but he's at the bottom of the list on RPRs.
runners.comment: Won Group 2 here in 2024 but has become tricky to place; bottom rated on RPRs
runners.owner: A Dal Pos, L Zanin, R Zanin

races.race_name: Lanwades Stud Nell Gwyn Stakes (Group 3) (Fillies)

runners.name: Fitzella
runners.spotlight: Group form last summer comprised a success over 6f at Ascot flanked by good efforts at Royal Ascot and Deauville (7f); took a good hold and weakened in the Cheveley Park Stakes, suggesting she may not be crying out for this step back up in trip.
runners.comment: Useful filly (Group winner) but may not be crying out for this step back up in trip

runners.name: Beautify
runners.spotlight: Form dipped in the Cheveley Park Stakes here but she may well rebound and resume the progress shown at the Curragh, where she won a Group 2 and was a close second in 7f Group 1 behind a stablemate who is currently favourite for the 1,000 Guineas; remains of interest.
runners.comment: Holds particularly strong claims on her 7f Moyglare Stud Stakes effort; still of interest
runners.owner: Derrick Smith & Mrs John Magnier & Michael Tabor

runners.name: America Queen
runners.spotlight: Did well over 6f (on good/firmer) last season, scoring by a wide margin at Haydock before on the premises in the Lowther (Group 2) at York and Cheveley Park Stakes (Group 1) at Newmarket; one of the main form contenders in this first 7f attempt.
runners.comment: Above average over 6f last term; ran well in the Cheveley Park Stakes final start

runners.name: Act Of Kindness
runners.spotlight: 3,700,000gns yearling; half-sister to a Fillies' Mile winner; favourite for C&D maidens last autumn, finishing third behind Mubasimah then responding to pressure to get the job done (on good ground) next time, despite still seeming green; a potential Group filly, given her profile, and commands respect.
runners.comment: 3,700,000gns yearling; won the second of two attempts over C&D; potential Group filly

runners.name: Awaken
runners.spotlight: Below par in sole start on softer than good; 2yo form otherwise featured a 6f maiden win and two good efforts at Group level, namely second in the Albany at Royal Ascot and fourth in the Rockfel over C&D; could go well.
runners.comment: Second in the Albany at Royal Ascot and fourth in the C&D Rockfel; could go well

runners.name: Mubasimah
runners.spotlight: Frankel filly who posted a couple of sound front-running performances over C&D last autumn, winning a maiden event (good to firm) and a rallying second in the Oh So Sharp Stakes (Group 3; good ground); open to further improvement and has clear possibilities, especially if again able to dominate.
runners.comment: Won maiden prior to rallying second in the Oh So Sharp, both over C&D; likely player

runners.name: Inis Mor
runners.spotlight: Thrice-raced filly whose progressive form has plenty of substance; registered novice wins on the Newmarket July course (7f) prior to second of 16 in valuable conditions race at Longchamp (1m) on Arc weekend; may well rate higher still; very solid claims.
runners.comment: Scored twice on the July course then ran well in Longchamp contest; form has substance

runners.name: Asmeralda
runners.spotlight: Night Of Thunder filly who cost 640,000euros as a yearling; beat a reasonably solid yardstick by half a length, pair clear, in 7f AW event at Kempton four months ago; stable has landed this prize six times, most recently last year; promising contender.
runners.comment: Successful in Kempton AW event; stable has good record in this race; promising

runners.name: Azleet
runners.spotlight: Broadly progressive 6f form last season culminated in her second in Listed contest at Newmarket (good) when doing well considering she took a good hold and had to wait for a run; ideally needs to settle better upped in distance and this is a harder task.
runners.comment: Broadly progressive over 6f but ideally needs to settle better upped in trip

runners.name: Golden Palace
runners.spotlight: Not disgraced in Listed event at Newmarket (6f) on final 2yo start but remains possibly flattered by the figure she appeared to record here when sixth in the Cheveley Park Stakes and, on balance, faces a difficult task in this field.
runners.comment: Balance of form suggests this is a difficult assignment; upped in distance

runners.name: Cherry Baker
runners.spotlight: Won well at Southwell (6f, AW) to open her account at the fifth attempt but looks highly unlikely to follow up, being towards the bottom of this pack on bare figures and a filly who has no obvious Group potential.
runners.comment: Won well at Southwell but looks highly unlikely to follow up at this level

runners.name: Harlequin Breeze
runners.spotlight: Kept to the Newmarket tracks last year and steadily got the hang of things, belying 50-1 odds with close second in C&D novice the final time; faces a much stiffer task upped in class and is the only maiden in this field.
runners.comment: Close second in C&D novice when last seen but this is a much stiffer task

races.race_name: Scorthy Champ Standing At Barton British EBF Maiden Fillies' Stakes (GBB/IRE Incentive Race)

runners.name: Jazz Queen
runners.spotlight: 210,000euros yearling by Too Darn Hot; first (early April) foal; dam placed 5f/6f (RPR 70), sister to winners Flying Sparkle (5f 2yo/7f) and Pres Rapide (5f 2yo), half-sister to winners Winter Power (5f Group 1 Nunthorpe) and Hay Chewed (5f Listed), out of 6f 2yo winner; speedy pedigree and she was the most expensive of these at auction; has to be respected for a yard that won this in 2024 and also introduced a winning juvenile here yesterday.
runners.comment: 210,000euros Too Darn Hot yearling; April foal; speedy pedigree and yard won this in 2024

runners.name: Bint Archange
runners.spotlight: Ubettabelieveit 45,000euros yearling; sixth (early March) foal; half-sister to four winners including Forefront (French 6.5f/7f including 2yo; RPR 93), Taritino (7f-1m6f Flat including 2yo, 82; 2m/2m3f hurdle) and Diamont Katie (6f AW; 77); dam 5.5f-7.5f 2yo winner (78); Ryan Moore booked for this debut and yard already off the mark with this year's juveniles; betting to provide some telling clues.
runners.comment: Ubettabelieveit 45,000euros yearling; early March foal; appeals on paper with Moore booked

runners.name: Havana Lightning
runners.spotlight: By Havana Grey; 110,000gns foal; second (February) foal; dam 5f AW winner (RPR 68), sister to triple 6f Group 1 winner The Tin Man, half-sister to 6f Group 2 winner Deacon Blues, out of 6f/7f winning sister to useful 6f-1m winner Heretic; that's an interesting pedigree and William Buick is handed the reins for this introduction.
runners.comment: 110,000gns Havana Grey (February) foal; interesting pedigree; firm interest with Buick up

runners.name: Call Me Tomorrow
runners.spotlight: 60,000gns yearling by Minzaal; third (April) foal; dam 5.7f/6f winner (RPR 89), half-sister to smart 1m-1m6f winner Micro Manage and Listed winners Magnanimous (1m), Thornbrook (1m) and Seisai (7.4f 2yo), out of close relative of 1m4f Group 1 winner Mont Ormel; stable has won two of the last four editions of this and also bagged the Brocklesby last month; a likely type on her first go.
runners.comment: 60,000gns yearling by Minzaal; April foal; yard won 2 of the last 4 renewals; likely type

runners.name: Beauty Box
runners.spotlight: 95,000gns yearling by Space Blues; second (late April) foal; half-sister to 6f 2yo winner Peel Park (RPR 81); dam 1m3f AW winner (89), half-sister to 6f 2yo Listed winner Straight Answer, out of Group-placed 7f 2yo winning half-sister to 1m2f Group 1 winner Announce; respected yard and market check required on this introduction.
runners.comment: 95,000gns Space Blues yearling; late April foal; market check required for respected yard

runners.name: Lady Jemima
runners.spotlight: By St Mark's Basilica; third (early April) foal; dam 6f 2yo winner (including Group 1 Prix Morny; RPR 115), half-sister to Listed-placed 1m4f winner Climate Friendly, out of unraced half-sister to US 1m1f Grade 3 winner Gender Agenda, family of Oaks/St Leger winner User Friendly; slightly mixed messages on pedigree for this debut test but will need a market check.
runners.comment: By St Mark's Basilica; April foal; mixed messages on pedigree but will need a market check

runners.name: Magic Effort
runners.spotlight: Fifth (late March) foal; half-sister to 5f AW 2yo winner Share The Profits (RPR 66); dam 5f 2yo winner (100), half-sister to 5f winner Jonboy, is granddaughter of half-sister to 1m Group 2 winner Inchmurrin (dam of high-class 7f performer Inchinor); first runner for sire Good Effort, who won three Listed races for these connections.
runners.comment: First runner for sire Good Effort; late March foal; dam 5f 2yo winner

runners.name: Sea Of Sardinia
runners.spotlight: By Caturra; seventh (early March) foal; half-sister to four winners including Reward Smile (6f/7f including 2yo/HK/NZ; RPR 92), Demetrius (6f 2yo; 75) and Aish (5f; 67); dam placed 5f/6f (including 2yo; 68), out of half-sister to Group 1 sprint winners Airwave and Jwala; yard was 1-9 in 2yo races last year.
runners.comment: By Caturra; early March foal; might be best watched unless market suggests otherwise

races.race_name: Molson Coors British EBF Maiden Fillies' Stakes (GBB Race)

runners.name: Lyrics Of Life
runners.spotlight: Registered her second-place finishes behind useful sorts who subsequently ran well in the Fillies' Mile, then posted a good effort on bare figures in French Group 3 (started favourite; again over 1m) on final 2yo run; the form pick.
runners.comment: Second-place finishes have been franked; good fifth in French Group 3; the form pick

runners.name: Star Of Hope
runners.spotlight: 2,900,000gns yearling; sister to two winners, notably Charyn (6f/1m including Group 1; RPR 127); dam Listed-placed 5f scorer (99); powerful yard has landed this race with debutantes in two of last three years; very interesting contender.
runners.comment: 2,900,000gns yearling; yard has landed this race with newcomers in two of last three years

runners.name: Darzah
runners.spotlight: 375,000gns yearling; by Dubawi and first foal of 7f/9.5f winner (including AW Listed; RPR 109); major yard; one of three newcomers in the field; heed the market signals
runners.comment: 375,000gns yearling; by Dubawi out of an AW Listed winner; heed the market signals

runners.name: Silver Lake
runners.spotlight: 420,000gns yearling; seemed to find 7f too sharp, in keeping with pedigree, at Newmarket (behind Capichera) in sole 2yo start; open to improvement over this longer distance.
runners.comment: Seemed to find 7f too sharp at Newmarket in sole 2yo start; open to improvement

runners.name: Lady Fizz
runners.spotlight: Expert Eye filly; dam 1m2f AW scorer (RPR 70), out of French 6.5f winning half-sister to Dubai World Cup winner Monterosso; wears hood on debut; best watched.
runners.comment: Expert Eye filly; dam AW scorer; wears hood on debut; best watched

runners.name: So Regal
runners.spotlight: Siblings include a 1m Listed scorer for her connections; beaten favourite but showed promise with close third, clear of remainder, at Chelmsford (7f, AW) in the autumn; looks sure to progress this term; respected.
runners.comment: Close third, clear of remainder; at Chelmsford; looks sure to progress; respected

runners.name: Capichera
runners.spotlight: Twice-raced filly who may take another step forward; finished well for fourth over 7f at Newmarket in November then kept on for close second over 1m at Chelmsford (AW); possibilities.
runners.comment: Finished fourth at Newmarket then kept on for close second at Chelmsford; possibilities

runners.name: Summer In Paris
runners.spotlight: Wootton Bassett filly whose autumn efforts comprised a promising fourth at Newmarket (7f) then lesser effort on AW; may show progress returned to turf with tongue-tie fitted.
runners.comment: Fourth at Newmarket prior to lesser effort on AW; may show progress returned to turf

races.race_name: Betway Best Odds Guaranteed Handicap (GBBPlus Race)

runners.name: High Storm
runners.spotlight: Markedly better this season when second at Southwell (1m3f, AW) and winning from the front in a maiden at Doncaster (1m2f, good to soft); has a competitive mark for this handicap debut and he'll be staying on.
runners.comment: Won 1m2f Doncaster maiden; competitive mark for handicap debut and he'll be staying on

runners.name: Waterford Castle
runners.spotlight: Showed promise in three turf runs for Ralph Beckett last summer; turned over at 4-6 this year at Wolverhampton (AW) in a 7f maiden and 8.6f handicap, which were his best form and he's shaping as if worth a go at 1m2f but he needs to find extra.
runners.comment: Odds-on defeats on AW (7f/8.6f) this year were his best form but he needs to find extra

runners.name: Ranga Tang
runners.spotlight: Showed ability on good ground at Newmarket (1m) and Goodwood (1m1f) before his neck second of ten in a novice at Wolverhampton (8.6f, AW; made most) in November; will stay 1m2f+; cheekpieces go on and he has what looks a competitive mark for handicap/seasonal debut.
runners.comment: Neck second in AW novice (8.6f; made most) in November; will stay; cheekpieces go on

runners.name: Oratorical
runners.spotlight: Three months off before showing substantial improvement to win 12-runner novice at Nottingham (1m, heavy) in October; creditable sixth of seven in French Listed race on final outing; better is needed on this handicap/seasonal debut but it looks like he'll surely stay.
runners.comment: Won novice at Nottingham (1m, heavy) in October and it looks like he'll surely stay

runners.name: Impossible Mission
runners.spotlight: Sole turf run was debut (on good; last of nine); improved on handicap debut when seeing off his five rivals at Wolverhampton (upped to 9.4f, AW) in November, but ran poorly at Lingfield (1m2f, AW; raced widest and a bit freely) on last month's reappearance; needs to resume improvement.
runners.comment: 9.4f AW winner in November but ran poorly on last month's reappearance

runners.name: Evanesco
runners.spotlight: Close second in a Goodwood maiden (7f, good to soft) and dominant novice win at Salisbury (1m, heavy) were easily best of his five efforts as 2yo; two of the others were in France; needs to resume progress but will stay 1m2f and is one to consider.
runners.comment: 2nd at Goodwood (7f) and win at Salisbury (1m, heavy) were easily best of five 2yo races

runners.name: Tailgunner Joe
runners.spotlight: Fair form on varied turf as 2yo; rather better in his seven-race campaign at Meydan in December-March, including a 1m win and he shaped as if he stayed 1m1f on latest start; needs to find extra but, unlike most of these, there are no doubts over his fitness.
runners.comment: Has been running at Meydan; needs to find extra but there are no doubts over his fitness

runners.name: Archers Bay
runners.spotlight: Some improvement in maidens in the mud for Aidan O'Brien in the autumn; 30,000gns purchase and first-time cheekpieces (replacing blinkers) when getting off the mark at first time of asking for new yard in seven-runner novice at Wolverhampton (9.4f, AW) in December, making all; needs a chunk of improvement on this handicap debut but the trip should not be a problem.
runners.comment: Ex-Aidan O'Brien; made all in novice at Wolverhampton (9.4f, AW) in December; needs better

races.race_name: Watch Unibet Racing Postcast Novices' Hurdle (GBB Race)

runners.name: Le Beau Madrik
runners.spotlight: Posted a very eyecatching RPR when making it 2-2 over hurdles at Newcastle in January (2m4f, soft; fine on good) and reportedly ran flat when only 7-2 for a Grade 2 at Kelso the following month; granted a break since then and he returns with his yard firing in winners left, right and centre.
runners.comment: 2-2 over hurdles until flat performance at Kelso; back from a break with yard riding high

runners.name: Matty's Getaway
runners.spotlight: Irish point winner who has been keeping good company over hurdles and, in a first-time tongue-tie, he comfortably accounted for subsequent Grade 2 winner Dalston Lad in a Doncaster maiden in late November (2m5f, good to soft); has since undergone a wind operation; major player provided the absence doesn't hold him back.
runners.comment: Took a notable scalp in November; major player provided the absence doesn't hold him back

runners.name: Padre Arthur
runners.spotlight: Only sixth in his first two races (debut was a bumper) but raised his profile when chasing home a talented and shorter-priced stablemate at Kempton in early February (2m5f, good to soft); he was 15l adrift but this 5yo no doubt has plenty more to offer.
runners.comment: Beaten 15l into second at Kempton but the winner is useful and that was better

runners.name: Legendary Luke
runners.spotlight: 3m point winner; relinquished his unbeaten record over hurdles in a 3m Grade 2 here in February but that was no backward step and he'd have finished closer than 12l to the winner Dalston Lad but for hitting two out; heading in the right direction and respected.
runners.comment: Relinquished his unbeaten record over hurdles in a 3m Grade 2 here in February

races.race_name: Unibet Abram Mares' Novices' Hurdle (Listed Race) (GBB Race)

runners.name: White Noise
runners.spotlight: Tongue tied for her last three starts and it's made a difference; after her defeat at Warwick (2m3f, heavy) she didn't look an obvious candidate for the Grade 2 over 2m1f at the Cheltenham Festival (good), but in the van throughout she dug deepest when it mattered to spring a 40-1 surprise; there was no fluke about it and the third has since finished runner-up in a Grade 1; carries a penalty back at Listed level.
runners.comment: No fluke about her Cheltenham win and that form has already been franked

runners.name: Torpille Dagrostis
runners.spotlight: Cheekpieces added for her last three races; it wasn't pretty when making hard work of justifying short odds in a Leopardstown maiden for mares in early March (2m5f, soft) but her heart seems in the right place and should run well again.
runners.comment: It wasn't pretty in winning her maiden but her heart seems in the right place

runners.name: Dream Shadow
runners.spotlight: Has just over 5l to find with White Noise on their Warwick clash in February (2m3f, heavy) but a 5lb pull with the winner can only help her cause and perhaps 3m on soft ground stretched her when a remote second at Doncaster (Listed); has to enter the reckoning.
runners.comment: 3m perhaps too far last time; previous run behind White Noise offers hope
runners.owner: M Doocey, S Doocey & P J Doocey

runners.name: Just A Glance
runners.spotlight: Won a point bumper and followed up in a 2m4f maiden; unbeaten over hurdles after minor wins over 2m at Hereford (soft) and Chepstow (good to soft); should stay this longer trip fine but on the face of it these terms look tough.
runners.comment: 2-2 over hurdles; should be fine over the longer distance but these terms are tough

runners.name: Livingonaprayer
runners.spotlight: Won mares' novice races at Huntingdon (2m3f, good) and Wincanton (2m5f, good to soft) before pulling up when 18-1 for a Listed event at Doncaster (3m, soft); beaten too far out to blame the longer trip but it was a miserable day in South Yorkshire and plenty of horses underperformed on that card; could be given another chance.
runners.comment: Dual winner; Doncaster was disappointing but probably wise to put a line through that

runners.name: Great Dance
runners.spotlight: Won a weak bumper at Newton Abbot on debut but was then well held in a Listed race and the form of her close third on hurdle debut at Taunton is nothing to shout about (2m, good); bred for this far but she'd be a surprise winner; tongue-tie added.
runners.comment: Bred for this longer distance but she'd be a surprise winner; tongue-tie added

runners.name: Girl Friday
runners.spotlight: Bought for £45,000 after winning a four-runner Irish mares' maiden point (3m, good); raced prominently and came home 4l to the good in a 2m3f maiden for mares at Wetherby; she's raised in class with little experience behind her but clearly useful.
runners.comment: She's raised in class with little experience behind her but clearly useful
runners.owner: Old Gold Racing With Francesca Cumani II

runners.name: Millady
runners.spotlight: Improved to be third in a maiden at Hereford (2m3f, good) but that form gives her no right to be troubling the best of these.
runners.comment: Form of Hereford third gives her no right to be troubling the best of these

races.race_name: Unibet Silver Trophy Handicap Chase (Grade 2) (A Limited Handicap) (GBB Race)

runners.name: Triple Trade
runners.spotlight: Still on a good mark despite winning twice this season (2m/2m4f; soft/good) and was 11-2 for the veterans' final over C&D earlier this month, only to depart early; has a lot in his favour so merits each-way consideration.
runners.comment: Early casualty here 11 days ago; has a lot in his favour so merits each-way consideration

runners.name: The Bluesman
runners.spotlight: Has won two of his four chases since joining Olly Murphy and impressed over C&D (soft) in February; an 8lb rise appeared to anchor him in the Jack Richards at Cheltenham, though good ground could have been another factor in him being below par (seems ideally served by softer); respected but one or two others appeal more.
runners.comment: Progressive chaser but on drying ground one or two others appeal more

runners.name: Outlaw Peter
runners.spotlight: Multiple winner who reacted well to the new cheekpieces when a clearcut winner of the Middle Distance Veterans' Final over C&D only 11 days ago; historically well treated despite the 6lb rise but there's a suspicion that younger limbs this time may have his measure.
runners.comment: Back up 6lb for his clearcut recent win in the Middle Distance Veterans' Final over C&D
runners.owner: The Stewart Family, Dench, Ferguson & Mason

runners.name: Moon d'Orange
runners.spotlight: 9lb higher when beaten 3.5l by Riskintheground 12 months ago; 0-10 in the meantime but the new visor worked well when a never-nearer third in the Plate at Cheltenham last month and clear claims if he can back that up.
runners.comment: The new visor worked well when a never-nearer third in the Plate last month

runners.name: Zurich
runners.spotlight: Could have done without this race being transferred from Cheltenham as he won there in October (good ground) and stayed on well for fifth in last month's Plate; his run in between at Doncaster in a race that's worked out well was also admirable; every chance.
runners.comment: Had a good season and his fifth in last month's Plate at Cheltenham was admirable

runners.name: Jour d'Evasion
runners.spotlight: Completed this season's hat-trick over shorter trips, in lower grades and on softer surfaces; won over a similar trip to this over hurdles but on soft ground that seems to suit him ideally; a good-ground renewal of the Grand Annual proved far too tough last month.
runners.comment: May have more to offer as a chaser but all his best form is on slower surfaces

runners.name: Riskintheground
runners.spotlight: Won this race 12 months ago at Cheltenham when 1lb higher so he's dangerously handicapped for his defence; his form had nosedived after winning in a small field at Newton Abbot in October but the signs were more encouraging last time in the 2m4f Plate at the Festival as he was staying on when hampered by a faller at the last fence; has to be of interest, especially given the yard's form.
runners.comment: Won this last year off 1lb higher and signs of returning to form last time at Cheltenham

runners.name: Fugitif
runners.spotlight: No win since late 2023 and he hasn't got a lot going this season; he had run well but looked on the retreat when unseating in Outlaw Peter's race here 11 days ago (visor replaced usual cheekpieces).
runners.comment: Looked on the retreat when unseating in Outlaw Peter's race here 11 days ago

runners.name: Bleu d'Enfer
runners.spotlight: Back from wind surgery he bumped into one well handicapped at Bangor two weeks ago (2m4f, good); it could be a similar story here and winning this would demand a career best from him.
runners.comment: Good run last time at Bangor but winning this would demand a career best from him

runners.name: Tommy's Oscar
runners.spotlight: Veteran who made his name as a two-miler and the slow pace helped when making his first foray over 3m a successful one at Carlisle (good to soft; effective on good); solid second the run before over 2m4f but a 5lb rise for last time could be troublesome.
runners.comment: The drop back in trip is no cause for concern but a 5lb rise for that last win has to be

runners.name: Mousey Brown
runners.spotlight: Has faced stiff tasks over hurdles on her last two starts in races won by Wodhooh and Feet Of A Dancer; has a bit to prove on the wellbeing front as her runs before those (chasing and hurdling) weren't her best, but both her chase wins have been on good ground at about this trip and this Irish raider now goes in cheekpieces.
runners.comment: Irish raider for whom trip/ground is ideal; revival needed but cheekpieces are new

races.race_name: Try Unibets Smartview Racecards Handicap Hurdle (GBB Race)

runners.name: Huffin An A Puffin
runners.spotlight: Won a Newbury novice hurdle in March 2024 (for Harry Derham) and returned from a long absence to make successful stable/handicap debut at Doncaster (2m3f, soft), beating none other than last week's Grade 1 winner Storming George; was found to be coughing after pulling up off this 3lb higher mark at Newbury (2m4f; favourite); interesting after wind surgery; should be well handicapped.
runners.comment: The defeat of Storming George now looks good; excuses last time; had wind surgery

runners.name: Analiese
runners.spotlight: Sole defeat in her last four races came at the hands of a progressive horse at Newbury (2m4f, good to soft); shrugged aside that minor blip with a workmanlike win at Chepstow (2m3f, soft); 3lb rise isn't the end of the world.
runners.comment: Progressive mare who has won three of her last four races; up just 3lb for the latest

runners.name: La Pinsonniere
runners.spotlight: Runner-up in a Listed hurdle for mares at this fixture (Cheltenham) 12 months ago; only 1-12 but she's likeably consistent and was third of 13 off this mark over Kempton's 2m5f a month ago (good) having been given every chance; solid claims once more.
runners.comment: Likeably consistent and was third of 13 off this mark over Kempton's 2m5f a month ago

runners.name: Royal Infantry
runners.spotlight: Skelton runner on a losing run of nine; up and down over hurdles this season and tried chasing the once in December; latest Kempton run when behind some of these wasn't overly encouraging and his trainer now turns to cheekpieces; still couldn't put a line through him.
runners.comment: Last run at Kempton not that encouraging but hot trainer now turns to cheekpieces

runners.name: Below The Radar
runners.spotlight: Over two years since he won a race but latest third at Uttoxeter (2m4f) looks all the better now with the winner Wellington Arch going in again last week at Aintree; this 8yo might have a preference for slightly slower ground but he's respected.
runners.comment: Latest Uttoxeter third was franked last week; respected, but may prefer slower ground

runners.name: Norman Fletcher
runners.spotlight: All five wins have been at about 2m or 2m1f; wasn't that strong through the line over Bangor's extended 2m3f last time (good) and has further to go here; tries cheekpieces.
runners.comment: Wasn't that strong through the line over Bangor's extended 2m3f last time

runners.name: Irandando Has
runners.spotlight: Very low mileage for a 7yo; makes handicap debut after going 2-3 for this yard and the horse he beat in a Newcastle novice last time (2m1f, good to soft) has won since, albeit at odds-on; this gelding retains significant potential, especially over this longer trip.
runners.comment: 2-3 for this yard and brings significant potential into handicaps; raised in distance
runners.owner: The Late John Hales And Miss L Hales

runners.name: Masked Dance
runners.spotlight: Better than ever in her last three starts over 2m5f and 3m; highly likely to run another solid race but this is stronger than she's accustomed to.
runners.comment: Highly likely to run another solid race but this is stronger than she's accustomed to

runners.name: Idy Wood
runners.spotlight: Won over fences last season and has won twice over hurdles since September; now he's on a recovery mission after very quiet runs at Windsor (2m4f) and at the Cheltenham Festival (3m; again on his favoured good ground).
runners.comment: Capable 8yo but on a recovery mission after finishing tailed on his last two starts

runners.name: Johnson's Blue
runners.spotlight: Tailed off on his final hurdle start in November and three subsequent chase runs leave him with further questions to answer; potentially on a useful mark if he can get back on track, but there are clearly risks attached.
runners.comment: Potentially on a useful mark if he can get back on track, but there are risks attached

runners.name: George's Lad
runners.spotlight: Has only won a maiden but there has been promise in his handicap runs and he caught the attention of the stewards when trying this far for the first time at Kempton on Boxing Day (good; off since); he did take a fierce hold on that occasion and will need to settle better here, but still interesting on the back of a wind operation.
runners.comment: Needs to settle better than at Kempton; retains potential at this trip; had wind surgery

races.race_name: Try Unibets New Racing Betbuilder Handicap Chase (GBB Race)

runners.name: Fierce Warrior
runners.spotlight: The furthest he's been was 3m2f when he rallied to win from Red Delta in a small field at Doncaster (soft; made most) in November; something amiss next time but he was back on song for second at Carlisle (3m, good to soft; probably effective on good) on latest outing, making it good runs on both his starts in cheekpieces; up 2lb but needs a close look.
runners.comment: Furthest he's been is 3m2f when rallying to beat Red Delta at Doncaster (soft) in November

runners.name: Some Scope
runners.spotlight: Won from the front in first-time visor (kept since) at Doncaster in December; back to form when second at Newbury on latest start, his second run after his second wind operation; has a competitive mark but his inconsistency and going beyond 3m2f both introduce doubts.
runners.comment: Has a competitive mark but his inconsistency and going beyond 3m2f both introduce doubts

runners.name: Bodhisattva
runners.spotlight: Third of five finishers in the 3m7f Edinburgh National at Musselburgh in February, which wasn't as good as his second in the same race in 2025; heavy defeats on last three outings but soft ground may not have been ideal.
runners.comment: Stays well; heavy defeats on last three outings but soft ground may not have been ideal

runners.name: Holokea
runners.spotlight: Won bumper and maiden hurdle when on good ground; scored at Ffos Las (3m1f, soft) in October and runner-up in four of his other six chases, including when mown down late on in a big field at Windsor (extended 3m4f, soft) in January; ran poorly over C&D next time and well beaten at Cheltenham Festival on latest but both those races were much warmer than this.
runners.comment: Mown down late on in big field at Windsor (extended 3m4f, soft) in January three runs back

runners.name: Ki Woo
runners.spotlight: Sent chasing this season and has been inconsistent (made a respiratory noise on penultimate start) but it looks as if he is best away from soft/heavy ground and won in clearcut fashion in a five-runner C&D race (good to soft) six weeks ago; 6lb higher today but 5lb claimer is recruited and he has more to offer as a stayer.
runners.comment: Won in clearcut fashion in a five-runner C&D race (good to soft) six weeks ago; 6lb higher

runners.name: Gold Emery
runners.spotlight: All starts on softer than good; no win from 16 attempts over fences but runner-up in seven, including off this mark when he tried an extended 3m6f in the North Wales National at Bangor on penultimate start; ran creditably over 3m7f at Chepstow latest, so has place claims again but he does not look the percentage call for win purposes.
runners.comment: Running creditably and stays, but no win from 16 attempts over fences (runner-up in seven)

runners.name: Red Delta
runners.spotlight: Stays thoroughly, having won the Lincolnshire National at Market Rasen (3m4f) on Boxing Day and performed well in the Eider at Newcastle (4m1f) in February; perhaps his heavy defeat over 3m on latest start can therefore be forgiven, particularly as he was hampered early on; considered back at 3m4f.
runners.comment: Stays thoroughly and latest start (3m/hampered) can be forgiven; considered back at 3m4f

runners.name: Yes Day
runners.spotlight: He's now 13lb below his peak mark but hasn't been at his best this season and didn't show much when back on good ground (3m) three weeks ago; won over 3m7f last March but there may be more solid options.
runners.comment: Below form this term and didn't show much on good ground latest; won over 3m7f last March

runners.name: The Grey Man
runners.spotlight: None too consistent this season but he did win in small fields at Ffos Las (3m, heavy; made all) and Wetherby (3m, good to soft) last month; this field is much more demanding company on his first venture beyond 3m.
runners.comment: Small-field wins last two starts; much more demanding company on this first go beyond 3m

runners.name: Regarde
runners.spotlight: Has had three wind operations, two of them last year; won four-runner race over C&D (good) when upped in trip last March and ran creditably on next two starts; not disgraced at Ascot in November when last seen; has a good record when returning from a break, so goes on the shortlist.
runners.comment: Won over C&D (good) last March; has a good record when returning from a break; shortlisted

runners.name: Fortunedefortunata
runners.spotlight: Mostly campaigned in competitive staying chases when with Gordon Elliott and did not accomplish much this season; £3,000 buy in February but fell mid-race here 11 days ago on debut for new yard; cheekpieces (tried once) return; major questions to answer.
runners.comment: Out of form for Gordon Elliott; fell mid-race here 11 days ago on debut for new yard

races.race_name: Read Nicky Hendersons Unibet Blog Handicap Hurdle (GBB Race)

runners.name: Kilwaughter
runners.spotlight: Smart in bumpers and getting there over hurdles after digging deep for a successful handicap debut under Jack Stenhouse at Newbury (2m4f, good to soft); 7lb rise seems harsh enough but he'll probably improve again for going 3m.
runners.comment: 7lb rise for last time seems harsh enough but he'll probably improve again for going 3m

runners.name: Smokeringinthedark
runners.spotlight: Maiden winner who opened his account in handicaps with a comfortable win at Newcastle (2m4f, good to soft) two weeks ago; a 3lb rise seems fair for all that it wasn't a strong race, and his yard can do little wrong right now.
runners.comment: Only 3lb higher than at Newcastle and his yard can do little wrong right now

runners.name: King Of The Lake
runners.spotlight: Four wins in 2025 and now only 2lb higher than for the last of those at Chepstow in December (2m7f, good to soft); however, he was a 33-1 chance last month at Uttoxeter and he ran like one.
runners.comment: He was a 33-1 chance last month at Uttoxeter and he ran like one

runners.name: Firestream
runners.spotlight: Maiden/handicap wins for Anthony Honeyball; long absences a feature in his profile but there were positives at Uttoxeter last month (2m4f, soft; first-time cheekpieces) where he ran well for a long way; stays this far; chance if the wheels can stay on.
runners.comment: Very lightly raced at nine; good run last time and chance if the wheels can stay on

runners.name: Serious Chat
runners.spotlight: The winner of three handicaps, at 2m7f, 2m6f and 2m3f; probably ran as well as he ever has when beaten half a length at Doncaster last time (3m, soft); thereabouts.
runners.comment: Probably ran as well as he ever has when beaten half a length at Doncaster last time

runners.name: Moon Rocket
runners.spotlight: Chasing this season and it started well enough, but heavy defeats on his last two starts (Grade 2/Cheltenham handicap) have prompted this return to hurdles; also has smart form in this discipline so it could be an inspired move; second run after wind surgery and in a tongue-tie.
runners.comment: Heavy defeats over fences have prompted this return to hurdling; useful in this scene

runners.name: Lord Snootie
runners.spotlight: Only 1-17 but has flashes of smart form in good handicaps and easily his best run for ages when a rallying second at Bangor last month (2m7f, good; effective on softer); weighted to win races and seriously considered.
runners.comment: His best run for ages when a rallying second at Bangor last month; weighted to win races

runners.name: Red Dirt Road
runners.spotlight: He can have his off days but he's 4-12 over hurdles and he ran well to be fourth in a Pertemps qualifier over C&D before dropping out in the final furlong after racing up with the pace; he was always likely to prove a sitting duck when it mattered at Cheltenham and better can be expected here.
runners.comment: A sitting duck when ridden prominently in the Pertemps Final; better can be expected

runners.name: Red Risk
runners.spotlight: Retains lots of ability despite his advancing years and won a Pertemps qualifier at Musselburgh in February (3m, good to soft); Uttoxeter was disappointing in a race he won the previous year, but he's shown bouncebackability in the past.
runners.comment: Uttoxeter was disappointing but he's shown bouncebackability in the past
runners.owner: Middleham Park Racing Xliv & A&j Ryan

runners.name: Dartan
runners.spotlight: Useful hurdler in Ireland; beaten in a match over fences on stable debut (first-time visor); this return to hurdles is welcomed but he appears to have developed some of his own ideas; had wind surgery.
runners.comment: Early days with this yard but appears to have developed some of his own ideas

races.race_name: Unibet Handicap Chase (GBB Race)

runners.name: Grand Geste
runners.spotlight: Suffered two flops in his last three races but the latest was in the National Hunt Chase at Cheltenham; both wins since December have been here (3m1f/3m4f), latterly in the 11-runner Grand National Trial that always takes some winning; if on a going day then he would have strong claims, for all that softer ground would be preferred.
runners.comment: Strong form claims back on his favourite track; good ground would be a slight concern

runners.name: Bhaloo
runners.spotlight: All-or-nothing profile and it's been nothing on his last two starts, while even if he was able to rediscover some spark after another break, his form figures at 3m or beyond are PPP; hardly the safest option.
runners.comment: Even if back on song after a break his form figures at 3m or beyond are PPP

runners.name: Iron Bridge
runners.spotlight: A strong stayer but with a strike-rate of only 2-17 over fences and excuses are wearing thin; on the plus side, they were solid enough efforts on last two starts (3m4f/3m6f) and he nearly won off this mark on his reappearance (3m2f).
runners.comment: Strong stayer but with a strike-rate of only 2-17 over fences and excuses are wearing thin

runners.name: Hold Your Fort
runners.spotlight: Did well in the autumn and won again last month at Ascot (3m, good) where he dominated his two rivals from the front; there's a suspicion that his two rivals underperformed and he's 5lb higher here, but still considered.
runners.comment: Dominated his two rivals from the front at Ascot (3m, good); up 5lb; respected

runners.name: Hang In There
runners.spotlight: Strike-rate of 9-27 over fences and his third in a veterans' chase at Newbury in February (3m2f) was encouraging given he had been absent for three months and good to soft ground would have been slow enough; should run well.
runners.comment: Encouraging third at Newbury last time and this ground should be more suitable

runners.name: Egbert
runners.spotlight: C&D winner in December 2024 (heavy; won on good) and made it 2-2 here when beating three rivals over 3m4f (good to soft) on reappearance; the glaring concern is his nothing run at Exeter last time when in trouble a long way out.
runners.comment: 2-2 here but the glaring concern is his nothing run at Exeter last time

runners.name: Minella Crooner
runners.spotlight: Once very smart for Gordon Elliott but left that yard on a downer and has continued in similar vein for current team; too risky at the minute.
runners.comment: Left Gordon Elliott on a downer and has continued in similar vein for current team

runners.name: Millforce
runners.spotlight: Absent since October when he signed off for Gavin Cromwell with a 2m6f win at Galway (yielding); stays 3m and should get this far, but would need to see some confidence behind him.
runners.comment: Absent since October when he signed off for Gavin Cromwell with a 2m6f win at Galway

races.race_name: Unibet Pony Racing Authority Graduates Handicap Hurdle (Conditional Jockeys' And Amateur Riders')

runners.name: Across Earth
runners.spotlight: Consistent for a while now and, although beaten off this mark on his last two starts, it was a pretty good handicap in which he went down narrowly at Newbury the last time (2m, good to soft); thereabouts if gelling with Jude Fernandes.
runners.comment: It was a pretty good handicap in which he went down narrowly at Newbury the last time

runners.name: Count Of Vendome
runners.spotlight: Dual bumper winner who hasn't been out of the first two over hurdles and his defeat two runs back was at the hands of a useful horse; odds of 1-4 last time and that win told us little, but he brings momentum into this handicap debut.
runners.comment: Very solid start over hurdles and he brings momentum into this handicap debut

runners.name: Brendas Asking
runners.spotlight: Won a handicap hurdle at Ludlow in October 2024 (2m, good) and the following April a novice back at the same venue, after flying too high at the Cheltenham Festival; has the form to feature off this mark if on her game after a year out.
runners.comment: Has the form to feature off this mark if on her game after a year out
runners.owner: Dr David Carey (Not The Turf Club)

runners.name: Scintillante
runners.spotlight: 5lb out of the weights and 50-1 when well beaten in the Welsh Champion Hurdle on final run in October; his earlier races were more realistic than that and he ran well; solid chance if fitness doesn't hold him back.
runners.comment: Outclassed on final run in October; he had been running well prior to that

runners.name: Innamorato
runners.spotlight: Placed on the Flat and over hurdles in France; it was a quiet start for Paul Nicholls but came good in a heavy-ground maiden at Taunton (2m3f) and then ran third in a slowly run race at Newbury (good to soft; form franked); open to improvement in a race of this nature (handicap debut), though he did swerve a recent Ascot engagement on account of good ground so watch the weather.
runners.comment: Probably improving and interesting on handicap debut if okay on this quicker ground

runners.name: Lively Citizen
runners.spotlight: Very capable over fences but pulled up in that discipline on his last two starts following a break of 217 days; he did go close over hurdles at Aintree three runs ago so there's a big effort in him if he can get his act together.
runners.comment: There's a big run in him back over hurdles if he can get his act together
runners.owner: Mark E Smith And Keith M Hawes

runners.name: Vocito
runners.spotlight: Four-time winner over hurdles but exposed and was performing no more than okay when last seen in November; however, he could be all the better for a break and he's on his last winning mark.
runners.comment: Exposed but could be all the better for a break and he's on his last winning mark

races.race_name: Irish Stallion Farms EBF Fillies Maiden

runners.name: Eretria
runners.spotlight: Purchased for 420,000gns as a yearling; beaten 0.75l behind stablemate at Dundalk on debut two weeks ago over an extended 1m2f; holds entries for English/Irish Oaks; race-fitness on her side and a danger to all if transferring form to turf.
runners.comment: Good second behind stablemate on AW debut and danger to all if transferring that to turf

runners.name: Sangreal
runners.spotlight: Camelot filly; 270,000gns yearling; half-sister to winners Roi De France (Listed-placed 1m inc AW; RPR 112) and Vaguely Royal (1m4f Flat inc AW, 100; 1m7f hurdle); dam 1m/8.5f winner (inc AW 2yo/US turf stakes; 97), closely related to 1m Group 1 winner; interesting newcomer who holds Classic entries.
runners.comment: Half-sister to two winners inc' Roi De France (RPR 112); interesting newcomer

runners.name: Altura
runners.spotlight: Closely related to 1m5f winner Sea Master (RPR 72); dam 1m3f and 1m4f (Listed) winner (102), half-sister to 1m2f Group 3 winner Alla Speranza and 1m1f/1m2f Listed winner Almeric, is granddaughter of Champion Stakes winner Alborada; other newcomers look a little more appealing on paper.
runners.comment: Dam 1m3f and 1m4f (Listed) winner (102); other newcomers look a little more appealing

runners.name: Knowinmeknowinyou
runners.spotlight: Stayed on for third behind easy winner Amelia Earhart on debut in October (1m, yielding) and the runner-up of that race has won since; step up in trip looks like the right move; holds Oaks entries and looks like a big player.
runners.comment: Stayed on for third on debut (1m) and runner-up has won since; big player
runners.owner: J Carthy & Mrs P Shanahan & Mrs J Magnier & R A Bartlett

runners.name: Pink Coral
runners.spotlight: One of three for trainer; Wootton Bassett filly; sister to 11.3f winner Drama Queen (RPR 92), half-sister to three winners inc Rain Goddess (7f/1m1f inc 2yo/Group 3; Irish Oaks runner-up; 112) and Queenstown (Listed-placed 1m4f; 112); dam placed 6f 2yo (80), out of 1,000 Guineas winner; looks like stable first-string for debut so entitled to respect.
runners.comment: Wootton Bassett filly appears stable first-string on debut; respected.
runners.owner: Derrick Smith & Mrs John Magnier & Michael Tabor

runners.name: Serendipia
runners.spotlight: St Mark's Basilica filly; half-sister to winners Up Helly Aa (8.5f-1m7f inc Listed; RPR 107), Earendel (French 10.5f; 91) and Innervisions (French 7f/7.5f); dam 6f-1m2f winner (inc 2yo/Group 1; 116), half-sister to US 1m2f Grade 2 winner Innit; worth a market check for debut.
runners.comment: St Mark's Basilica filly; half sister to three winners inc' Up Helly Aa (RPR 107)

runners.name: Freedom And Light
runners.spotlight: Vast improvement needed on basis of two maiden runs to date; beaten 16l at Gowran last time (7f, soft).
runners.comment: Will need vast improvement to get involved here based on two m'den runs to date

runners.name: Camelot Queen
runners.spotlight: Camelot filly has 10l to find with Knowinmeknowinyou based on their clash here over 1m in October (yielding); newcomer Pink Coral appears to be the pick of the stable's three.
runners.comment: 10l to find with Knowinmeknowinyou based on their clash here in Oct'

runners.name: Musical Chimes
runners.spotlight: One of three for trainer; failed to beat a rival on debut over 1m here (yielding) when behind stablemate Camelot Queen and Knowinmeknowinyou; a fair bit to find on the basis of that.
runners.comment: A fair bit to find on basis of debut when behind Camelot Queen and Knowinmeknowinyou
runners.owner: Michael Tabor & Derrick Smith & Mrs John Magnier

races.race_name: Student Style Awards (C & G) Maiden

runners.name: Asakir
runners.spotlight: Did well as an apprentice-ridden second string on debut at the Curragh; made late headway into third, 1 1/4l behind stablemate Latin America (whose rider now takes over) and 7l clear of the fourth; holds a leading chance
runners.comment: Did well as an apprentice-ridden second string on debut, holds a leading chance
runners.owner: Exors Of The Late H H Aga Khan

runners.name: Cannes
runners.spotlight: Sea The Stars colt cost E500,000 as a yearling; dam placed 1m (52), half-sister to Derby/St Leger & Ascot Gold Cup runner-up Mojo Star; promising second in a 1m Curragh maiden in which the trainer had the first three, should go close
runners.comment: Second in a 1m Curragh maiden in which the trainer had the first three, should go close
runners.owner: Al Shaqab Racing & Stall Perlen AB

runners.name: Biennale
runners.spotlight: Fourth foal; half-brother to winners Angelo Buonarroti (7f 2yo) and Musical Score (US 1m turf); dam 6f/7f maiden, sister to Group 1 winners Churchill (Dewhurst/2,000 Guineas) and Clemmie (Cheveley Park).
runners.comment: Dam maiden, sister to Group 1 winners Churchill and Clemmie, talented apprentice aboard
runners.owner: Derrick Smith & Mrs John Magnier & Michael Tabor

runners.name: Chairmanforlife
runners.spotlight: E80,000 Y; fourth foal; half-brother to French 6f/6.5f 2yo winner Voodoo Magic (RPR 95); dam 5f AW 2yo winner (85), half-sister to useful 6f winners Boston Rocker and Mustallib, out of 5f 2yo winning half-sister to good 2yo sprinter Superstar Leo
runners.comment: Half-brother to French 2yo winner Voodoo Magic, dam 5f AW 2yo winner, yard's second string

runners.name: Gaelic Drills
runners.spotlight: Parish Hall colt is the first foal out of a 1m AW winner by Lawman; dam is out of unraced sister to 7f winner Solas Na Greine.
runners.comment: Parish Hall colt is the first foal out of a 1m AW winner by Lawman, stable 1-16 in 2026

runners.name: Shivoo
runners.spotlight: E75,000 Y; fourth foal; Sea The Stars colt is closely related to German 1m2f Listed winner Mercedes; dam German 1m2f Group 2 winner.
runners.comment: By Sea The Stars, closely related to a German Listed winner, dam German Group 2 winner

runners.name: Silver Cuff
runners.spotlight: E70,000 Y; fourth foal; half-brother to French 1m2f winner Rua; dam Listed-placed 1m winner,
runners.comment: Half-brother to a French 1m2f winner, dam Listed-placed winner, stablemate of Shivoo

runners.name: Wyman
runners.spotlight: Similar efforts in defeat in two starts over 1m and 7f last season; will need to have improved over the winter to score here; tongue-tie on.
runners.comment: Two fair runs last season but will need to have improved over the winter to score here

runners.name: Oh I Do Declare
runners.spotlight: Beat only one rival at Listowel last September, same story at Gowran last week; no apparent chance.
runners.comment: Beat only one rival at Listowel last September, same story at Gowran last week

runners.name: Antigua
runners.spotlight: Camelot colt cost 600,000gns as a yearling; slow to break, never counted on debut at this venue; beaten 19l in mid-field at the Curragh on second attempt; appears safely held by Cannes; however, a big step forward is not out of the question; Wayne Lordan aboard now.
runners.comment: Will need to take a big step forward from juvenile form, that is not out of the question

runners.name: Avec Grand Plaisir
runners.spotlight: Finished behind today's rival Antigua in mid-field at the Curragh last November; gelded now.
runners.comment: Finished behind today's rival Antigua in mid-field at the Curragh last November, gelded

races.race_name: "Domin OH HOO HOO" Handicap

runners.name: Coeur d'Or
runners.spotlight: Four-time winner; on a long losing sequence; towards the rear on seasonal debut at the Curragh; now 9lb below career-high mark but needs to show a spark of revival.
runners.comment: On a long losing sequence, towards the rear on seasonal debut at the Curragh
runners.owner: Mark E Phelan & S P O'Connor

runners.name: Abbey Actress
runners.spotlight: Likes soft ground and won off 9lb lower on her final start for John O'Donoghue at Navan (1m2f, soft) last April; holds a leading chance now after a good effort on seasonal/stable debut over 1m at the Curragh; met trouble in running, and her performance can be upgraded.
runners.comment: Won on soft early last season, promising effort on seasonal/stable debut at the Curragh

runners.name: Derry Lad
runners.spotlight: Four-time Flat winner at up to 1m4f; no sign of his old form in two outings at Dundalk since rejoining his original trainer
runners.comment: No sign of his old form in two outings at Dundalk since rejoining his original trainer

runners.name: Diamond Exchange
runners.spotlight: Placed form last year; second in a small-field C&D maiden last May; well beaten in third behind a useful filly at Down Royal; winning favourite in a Dundalk maiden in January; has potential for further progress in first-time cheekpieces.
runners.comment: Winning favourite in a Dundalk maiden in January, has potential for for further progress
runners.owner: Bronsan Racing & H Redknapp & John Breslin

runners.name: Diego El Queso
runners.spotlight: Proved frustrating for much of last season, beaten favourite in three maidens; off the mark at Dundalk in October; just denied a first turf win in a 21-runner contest at the Curragh 12 days ago; holds a leading chance
runners.comment: Just denied a first turf win in a 21-runner contest at the Curragh, holds a leading chance

runners.name: Thatwilldoso
runners.spotlight: Progressed really well last summer with wins at Roscommon and Galway; holding her form well to record seconds at Sligo and Naas; fair chance if her fitness holds up.
runners.comment: Made good progress in the second half of last season, fair chance if her fitness holds up

runners.name: Quatre Bras
runners.spotlight: Three-time AW winner; out of form on turf last season; enjoyed a revival at Dundalk in recent months and proved as effective on turf when second to Independent Expert on soft ground at Naas; plenty to find with Diego El Queso on Curragh running.
runners.comment: Good run at Naas last month, plenty to find with Diego El Queso on Curragh running

runners.name: Mr Rango
runners.spotlight: Three-time winner at 1m2f on soft or heavy; last success was in this race two years ago for a different stable; only flashes of form last season but interesting now off 10lb lower than last winning mark; out of his depth in the Irish Lincoln before showing signs of a return to form with a good run over 1m4f at the Curragh.
runners.comment: Won this race for a different stable in 2024, 10lb lower, interesting after Curragh fourth

runners.name: Amplitude
runners.spotlight: Rounded off last term in with an emphatic 6l win in Gowran maiden, seemingly improving for first try on very testing ground;  unfancied and ran accordingly on seasonal debut at the Curragh.
runners.comment: Maiden winner on final start last season, unfancied and ran accordingly on seasonal debut

runners.name: Dutch Gold
runners.spotlight: A long time since he showed his best form; the most recent of his two wins was in 2023; this season's two Curragh runs provide no encouragement; held by Mr Rango.
runners.comment: A long time since he showed his best form, two Curragh runs provide no encouragement
runners.owner: Albert Dravins & Exors of the Late Eamonn Scanlon

runners.name: Day Trader
runners.spotlight: Maiden hurdle winner on good ground at Listowel last summer; hard to assess on the limited evidence of his Irish Flat form; wins in France in 2024 were over longer trips on soft.
runners.comment: Maiden hurdle winner on good ground last summer, Flat wins in France were at longer trips

runners.name: War Rooms
runners.spotlight: Third in the 2024 Dante and generally kept good company on the Flat in Britain; started brightly in Ireland in the spring; failed to sustain that form; struggled in two maiden hurdle runs in December.
runners.comment: 2024 Dante third, has failed to prosper since a good start to last season, revival needed

races.race_name: Irish Stallion Farms EBF Heritage Stakes (Listed Race)

runners.name: The Lion In Winter
runners.spotlight: Impressed in a Curragh maiden and the Group 3 Acomb Stakes at two; failed to win in a high-profile campaign last year; flopped in the Dante and the Derby; redeemed himself with smart placed form in 1m Gr 1s at Longchamp/Ascot and in the Breeders' Cup Mile; never threatened in Hong Kong Mile; easy ground a worry, though he ran well on soft in the Prix Jean Prat.
runners.comment: Unbeaten at two, strong placed form at the highest level last season, ground a concern
runners.owner: Michael Tabor & Derrick Smith & Mrs John Magnier

runners.name: Ribee
runners.spotlight: Enjoyed a terrific spell last October, notching three wins and two places during a hectic spell; maintained his rate of improvement when beating 24 rivals in the Irish Lincoln on return; not as effective over an extended 1m2f in a Listed race at Naas; back to form quickly to take second in a 1m2f premier handicap at the Curragh; this is probably closer to his optimum trip.
runners.comment: Highly progressive in handicaps last October, has continued to prosper, this looks taxing

runners.name: Gaiety Girl
runners.spotlight: Placed all three starts before a recent AW maiden win; this is highly ambitious for a 75-rated filly making her turf debut.
runners.comment: Recent AW maiden winners; this is highly ambitious for a 75-rated filly on turf debut

runners.name: Lord Massusus
runners.spotlight: Seven career wins include this race last season, when he got up in the last strides to deny the odds-on favourite Mutasarref; produced his best subsequent effort when fourth in the Minstrel Stakes at the Curragh last July; dropped away tamely in the 7f Gladness Stakes at the Curragh on seasonal debut.
runners.comment: Seven career wins include this race last season (beating Mutasarref), in rear on return

runners.name: Norwalk Havoc
runners.spotlight: Four-time winner at 7f/1m, including two late-season Listed events at this venue on heavy ground; returned with fair seventh of 25 behind Ribee in the Irish Lincoln; appeared not to stay 1m2f in another premier handicap at the Curragh on latest
runners.comment: Has won two late-season C&D Listed events on heavy ground, faces stiff competition here

runners.name: Tokenomics
runners.spotlight: Five career wins, the most recent off 98 in a 1m handicap at Dundalk last November; should strip fitter following an AW run in February; stable in fine form, combined for a Dundalk treble last week.
runners.comment: Should strip fitter following an AW run in February, stable in fine form, place chance

runners.name: Jancis
runners.spotlight: Won 7f Group 3 at this venue in 2024; form last season included fifth in 1m Group 2 and 1m2f Group 1 events at the Curragh before fourth in Group 3 at Ascot; last of six in C&D Group 3 last August; good effort when second in a 1m Listed race at Dundalk before a winter break.
runners.comment: Won 7f Group 3 at this venue in 2024, ran several good races in defeat last season

runners.name: Mutasarref
runners.spotlight: Ten victories include four here, most notably twice at Group 3 level in 2024; not quite at the same level this year but solid form, most recently seasonal-best when getting up late for second in Group 2 Solonaway Stakes at the Curragh.
runners.comment: Dual course Group 3 winner in 2024, failed to win last season, respected on overall form

runners.name: Mississippi River
runners.spotlight: Consistent in handicaps and conditions races at around 1m/1m1f last season; only once out of the first four in his nine 2024 starts, three wins, including over C&D; has never run in a stakes race and will need to raise his game to score at this level.
runners.comment: Has never run in a stakes race and will need to raise his game to score at this level
runners.owner: D Smith & Mrs J Magnier & M Tabor & Peter M Brant

runners.name: Expanded
runners.spotlight: 2024 Dewhurst runner-up on soft; ran way below that level in two Guineas attempts on better ground last spring; faded after showing up early at Newmarket, always behind at the Curragh after a slow start; failed to run to expectations when unplaced favourite for a C&D Listed race last September.
runners.comment: 2024 Dewhurst runner-up, ran in two Classics last year, well beaten over C&D last autumn

runners.name: Tarima
runners.spotlight: Superb pedigree; landed the odds on debut in C&D  fillies' maiden early last season; failed to run to expectations in 1m2f Group 3 event at Navan in May; fair third in a Listed race at Gowran in August; satisfactory display in fourth in the Blandford Stakes.
runners.comment: C&D maiden winner on debut last year, ran well in defeat on final two 2025 outings
runners.owner: Exors Of The Late H H Aga Khan

runners.name: Cowardofthecounty
runners.spotlight: Won a 7f Group 3 event at Deauville in 2024; tried at Group 1 level after; struggled in three runs last season; tongue-tied for seasonal debut; best watched.
runners.comment: Won a 7f Group 3 event at Deauville in 2024, struggled in three runs last season

races.race_name: Irish Stallion Farms EBF Median Auction Fillies Maiden

runners.name: Caitouna
runners.spotlight: Two Dundlak displays have been respectable; will need to find more to win a turf maiden.
runners.comment: Two Dundalk displays have been respectable, will need to find more to win a turf maiden

runners.name: Lake Bonney
runners.spotlight: E30,000 yearling. 12th foal; half-sister by Tens Sovereigns to six winners, best of them After (also the dam of Group 2 winner Armory), and Temps Au Temps (7f 2yo Listed); dam a useful 7f-1m2f winner by Chanrdwood Forest.
runners.comment: 12th foal; half-sister by Tens Sovereigns to six winners, worth considering
runners.owner: Barry Fowler & Mrs A M O'Brien

runners.name: Mixed Feelings
runners.spotlight: Looked like a possible future when third over this trip at Gowran on her only start at two; only one length behind a Ballydoyle-trained winner who was later placed in a small-field Group 3 race.
runners.comment: Looked like a possible future when third over this trip at Gowran on her only start at two

runners.name: Coolangatta
runners.spotlight: Not a bad run at Cork on her second start at two; does not yet appeal as a likely winner; stablemate Lake Bonney has a senior rider aboard.
runners.comment: Does not yet appeal as a likely winner, stablemate Lake Bonney has a senior rider aboard

runners.name: Bang Bang
runners.spotlight: SLow to break but shaped nicely over 7f here on debut last July when staying on strongly into fifth, having run green; not beaten far when fifth of nine at the Galway festival; did not appear again and needs to find extra; stable in good form
runners.comment: Fair 7f run here and at Galway last season, needs to find a bit extra, stable in good form

runners.name: Ballerina Dancer
runners.spotlight: Fifth foal; half-sister by El Kabeir to two winners; dam 7f 2yo winner by Amadeus Wolf; a rare Flat runner for jumps yard.
runners.comment: Fifth foal, half-sister to two winners, dam 7f 2yo winner, rare Flat runner for jumps yard

runners.name: De Songbird
runners.spotlight: E15,000 yearling; eighth foal; half-sister to winners in the US and Qatar; dam 7f 2yo winner, sister to 7f/1m1f Group 3 winner Decado; decent pedigree; stable not typically associated with first-time-out winners.
runners.comment: Half-sister to winners in the US/Qatar, yard not typically associated with debut winners

runners.name: Miss Romagna
runners.spotlight: E11,000 yearling; third foal; dam unraced Galileo half-sister to 7f/1m winner Kinver Edge, out of US 1m Grade 3 winning sister to US 1m Grade 1 winner Jack Milton; , stable 2-33 in 2026
runners.comment: Dam unraced Galileo half-sister to 7f/1m winner Kinver Edge, stable 2-33 in 2026

runners.name: Moon Aura
runners.spotlight: E32,000 yearling; sixth foal; half-sister by Space Blues to winners for several leading stables; dam won the Fred Darling for Ralph Beckett.
runners.comment: Half-sister by Space Blues to winners, dam won the Fred Darling for Ralph Beckett

runners.name: Venerini
runners.spotlight: E15,000 yearling; sixth foal; half-sister to a Swiss winner; dam French middle distance winner, half-sister to the useful and versatile Simenon; stable better known for jumpers; best watched.
runners.comment: Half-sister to a winner in Switzerland, dam a French middle distance winner, best watched

runners.name: Squishy
runners.spotlight: Slowly away and always at the back first time out at Dundalk, safe to rule out.
runners.comment: Slowly away and always at the back first time out at Dundalk, safe to rule out

runners.name: Snow Princess
runners.spotlight: Mohaather filly cost E110,000 as a yearling; has shown enough in her two runs at Dundalk to suggest she can make her presence felt on turf debut.
runners.comment: Has shown enough in her two runs at Dundalk to suggest she can make her presence felt

runners.name: Premier Princess
runners.spotlight: Never in the hunt when a remote fifth in a 1m2f maiden at the Curragh on recent debut.
runners.comment: Never in the hunt when a remote fifth in a 1m2f maiden at the Curragh on recent debut

runners.name: Lady Kelly
runners.spotlight: Took a while to get going over 7f at the Curragh on debut last October; kept on late, should be more mature now.
runners.comment: Took a while to get going over 7f at the Curragh on debut, should be more mature now

races.race_name: Horseware Ireland Student Derby Handicap

runners.name: Zipster
runners.spotlight: Won his third race when taking a Killarney handicap over 1m last July off this mark; some solid runs since, including when third of 13 at Gowran last week (1m2f, soft); inside draw and good amateur aboard.
runners.comment: Won third race in July off this mark and solid runs since inc' when third latest

runners.name: Baila Conmigo
runners.spotlight: Progressing well on AW for current yard, recording a second win at Dundalk last month off 61 before solid third there next time; couldn't transfer that form to turf at Bellewstown last time when tailed off (1m4f, soft); perhaps better ground can suit more, but likelihood is that he's better on AW.
runners.comment: Progressing well on AW with two recent wins; tailed off back on turf last time

runners.name: Staywitherjohnny
runners.spotlight: Two of her maiden runs were promising based on current mark; however, her two recent handicap runs don't reflect that, finishing last of 15 at Galway (1m1f, soft) and 14l behind at Killarney (1m, soft) in cheekpieces.
runners.comment: Promise can be gleaned from m'dens but two handicap starts leave a lot to be desired

runners.name: Urban War
runners.spotlight: Won twice as a 2yo in Britain for Amy Murphy in 2020; fairly moderate runs over hurdles and on AW since returning from three-year hiatus; others preferred.
runners.comment: Moderate runs over hurdles and on AW since return from three-year hiatus; others preferred

runners.name: Star Mind
runners.spotlight: Turf win came at Naas last August off 62 (7f, good to yielding); decent enough efforts at Dundalk recently; place chance.
runners.comment: Turf win came at Naas last Aug' off 62; decent enough AW runs recently; place chance

runners.name: Your Out Of Line
runners.spotlight: Best run came in a Gowran claimer in 2024 when a 1.5l fourth (1m, soft); heavy defeats over hurdles this season preceded a similar story in a Curragh handicap last time (1m, heavy); best watched on stable debut.
runners.comment: Best watched on stable debut following heavy defeats over hurdles/Flat this year

runners.name: Some Good Men Gone
runners.spotlight: Hit the crossbar over an extended 1m2f at Naas (good to yielding) on handicap debut off 2lb higher; reasonable effort back in a maiden at Gowran next time when fourth; the heavy ground was perhaps not to his liking when last seen at Gowran when 23l behind; chance.
runners.comment: Hit the crossbar on h'cap debut; heavy ground not to his liking last time; chance

runners.name: Teffian Warrior
runners.spotlight: Best effort from five maidens came at Galway in July when a 6l fifth of 14 (7f, yielding); may not have appreciated softer ground when tailed off at Gowran and 4l behind at Roscommon; interesting contender on handicap debut
runners.comment: May appreciate better ground compared to last two m'den runs; interesting on h'cap debut

races.race_name: FM104 "Giddy Up" Handicap

runners.name: Hugo's Girl
runners.spotlight: 13-race maiden has run some solid races in defeat; took third of 15 in a Curragh fillies' handicap last season off 71 (7f, good); third over 1m at Dundalk last time when returning from a break was encouraging; hood goes on for first time; chance.
runners.comment: M'den has run some solid races; third for comeback run on AW last time was encouraging

runners.name: Thaloria
