runners.spotlight: Stepped up markedly on maiden runs when scoring on handicap debut at Dundalk in January over 7f; tailed off the following month off 7lb higher; good second in a claimer last time when conceding weight to better-rated rivals; capable at her best.
runners.comment: Some good AW runs inc' a h'cap win; capable at her best

runners.name: Free Solo
runners.spotlight: Added his sixth win when landing a Roscommon handicap in August (7.5f, good) off 6lb lower; not at his best in runs since and finished last of 13 at Dundalk last time.
runners.comment: Added sixth win at Roscommon in Aug'; not at best since and last of 13 on AW latest

runners.name: I Bid You Ajou
runners.spotlight: Strung three consecutive wins together last summer at Fairyhouse in a claimer and two handicaps on good ground; remains 2lb above his last winning mark but it was a very positive seasonal return at Dundalk two weeks ago when a 0.5l runner-up under today's rider; in the mix but won't want any rain.
runners.comment: Put three wins together last summer at Fairyhouse on good ground; second on AW last time

runners.name: Glory To Be
runners.spotlight: Navan maiden winner in October (5f, soft); good return to action at the Curragh last time when beaten a neck into second (keeping on well) over 6f on soft to heavy; up 3lb but can go well; high draw.
runners.comment: M'den winner went close on return to actipn at Curragh last time; up 3lb but can go well

runners.name: Senna's Girl
runners.spotlight: No wins from 12 but went close over 7f at the Curragh in November (heavy); raced from 5lb outside the handicap in the Irish Lincolnshire last time when tailed off (1m, heavy); tongue tie goes on now but others make more appeal.
runners.comment: Winless from 12 and tailed off in Irish Lincoln last time; others more appealing

runners.name: Vega's Muse
runners.spotlight: Added second turf win when keeping on well to comfortably land a 1m course handicap three days ago (1m, good to yielding); carries 7lb penalty now but big contender if this doesn't come too soon.
runners.comment: Comfortably landed a 1m h'cap here three days ago; big player under 7lb penalty

runners.name: Goal Exceeded
runners.spotlight: Dual turf winner with the latest of those coming on stable debut at the Curragh in May last year off 73 (7f, good); struggled off revised mark for the remainder of last season; first-time out could be the time to catch him; high draw.
runners.comment: Second turf win came on stable debut last season; first-time out may be time to catch him
runners.owner: Ms H McGuinness/Martin W White/Declan Sheridan/Breen White

runners.name: Thru And Thru
runners.spotlight: Some good runner-up finishes in maidens and was beaten just 1l at Naas for her handicap debut last month (7f, soft), led before being run down into third late on; runs off the same mark now and Colin Keane takes over; big player.
runners.comment: Beaten just 1l on h'cap debut last time; Keane takes over now and looks a big player

runners.name: Greek Flower
runners.spotlight: Back-to-back winner in 2023 in a 5f Navan maiden (soft) and a 6f Curragh handicap off 70 (heavy); nothing since then but creditable fifth at the Curragh in November on her first try at this trip in a first-time hood and tongue tie (left off now).
runners.comment: Nothing since back-to-back wins in 2023; creditable fifth on first try at 7f in Nov'

runners.name: Anvika
runners.spotlight: Turf and AW winner was 7.75l behind Vega's Muse at this venue three days ago when finishing in midfield (1m, good to yielding); this comes quick enough and others preferred.
runners.comment: This comes quick enough since finishing 7.75l behind Vega's Muse here last time

runners.name: Chester Nimitz
runners.spotlight: Won his final start for Noel Meade in a Navan maiden in October (6f, yielding) under a front-running ride; makes stable debut now and not discounted.
runners.comment: Won final start for Noel Meade in Navan m'den; not discounted on stable debut

runners.name: Finsceal Annie
runners.spotlight: Won two races within a week here last year over C&D on different ground types; some good efforts at Laytown and Dundalk since then; beaten 8l in an AW handicap when last seen in November (1m); 10lb higher since last winning mark but worth a look back at this track.
runners.comment: Won two C&D races within a week last year; worth a look back here off 10lb higher

runners.name: Pebble Island
runners.spotlight: One-time 200,000gns yearling whose second placing in a four-horse maiden at Redcar marked a highlight from seven starts for the Crisfords; sent hurdling for his last two runs for new yard with little success.
runners.comment: Second in four-horse m'den was best run for Crisfords; little success hurdling recently

runners.name: Proleek Prince
runners.spotlight: Punchestown handicap winner in September 2024 off this mark (1m, good) and added an AW win at Dundalk later that year; struggled on turf last season and not many positives in his 8l tenth at Dundalk when last seen in November.
runners.comment: Turf winner off this mark in 2024; struggled last term; not many positives in latest run

runners.name: Verhoyen
runners.spotlight: Seven-time winner on turf, but last season's campaign was fruitless; mark is at an all-time low but not getting any younger and record suggests he'll improve from his first time out.
runners.comment: Last season's campaign was fruitless; mark dropping but not getting any younger
runners.owner: Paul Cullen & Joseph E Keeling & M C Grassick

races.race_name: BuzzBallz Handicap

runners.name: Roman Harry
runners.spotlight: Gained just second win from 39 starts when springing a 50-1 shock in a Dundalk claimer in December (7f); creditable efforts in two handicaps back at that venue for new yard; others preferred.
runners.comment: 2-39 after 50-1 shock in AW claimer; creditable efforts in two h'caps for new yard since

runners.name: Methgal
runners.spotlight: Made the breakthrough in a 7f Dundalk handicap last month off 55 under a 7lb claimer and good effort to take second off this mark next time; in good form and capable if bringing his best to turf.
runners.comment: Shed m'den tag in an AW h'cap last month and good second off revised mark next time

runners.name: Kitty Bear
runners.spotlight: Made the breakthrough at the 17th attempt when winning a Dundalk 1m handicap in January under this rider (1m); strong in the market earlier this month when beaten a short head into second (1m); moderate strike rate lessens enthusiasm.
runners.comment: 1-20 making the breakthrough on AW in Jan'; moderate strike rate lessens enthusiams
runners.owner: Charles Kerwin & Brendan Kerwin & Liam Kehoe

runners.name: Mogwli
runners.spotlight: Eight-time winner, but 0-10 at this venue; a lot of improvement needed based on two runs Curragh runs this season.
runners.comment: Eight-time winner; a lot of improvement needed based on two Curragh runs this term

runners.name: Romantic War
runners.spotlight: Still a maiden after 22 outings; respectable run on final 2025 turf start when third of 15 at Galway (7f, heavy); tailed off at Bellewstown in first run this year (1m, soft); blinkers go on now.
runners.comment: Still a m'den after 22 outings; tailed off at Bellewstown last time

runners.name: Brains
runners.spotlight: Prolific AW winner in Britain but added a third turf win when successful in a big-field 1m handicap at this venue in March last year (good) off 4lb higher; getting on in years and was tailed off at Dundalk last time.
runners.comment: Added third turf win in 1m h'cap here last year; tailed off on AW last time

runners.name: Exceeding
runners.spotlight: Record of 1-28 is off-putting; posted an excellent display from 6lb outside the handicap at Naas on seasonal return when second of 15; let favourite backers down at Navan next time when never better than midfield; hard to trust.
runners.comment: Record of 1-28 is off-putting; excellent second at Naas, but didn't back it up last time

runners.name: Rock Basher
runners.spotlight: No wins from 21 races; sent off the 9-2 favourite at Gowran for stable debut under today's rider but weakened inside the final furlong to finish sixth of 16; perhaps capable of more with that run under his belt considering his strength in the market that day.
runners.comment: 0-21 but sent off fav' at Gowran when sixth; perhaps capable of more now

runners.name: Zabriskie Point
runners.spotlight: Both wins came in 2023 for Charles Hills at 5f/7f on different ground types; has plunged in the ratings since but doesn't look like adding to his tally based on 2026 runs, although he now enters 0-60 company.
runners.comment: Plunged in ratings since two wins for Charles Hills in 2023; enters 0-60 company now
runners.owner: B White & M W White & Ms H McGuinness & D Sheridan

runners.name: Twotolose
runners.spotlight: Close third in second maiden start in 2024 but hasn't progressed from there; little encouragement in either handicap start and returns from 315-day absence now.
runners.comment: Hasn't progressed and little encouragement in either h'cap start; back from 315 days off

runners.name: Cannonball Queen
runners.spotlight: Promise on Curragh debut last July when beaten just 5.5l (7f, good) but has been below that level since and beaten 10l on handicap debut when last seen in October (7f, yielding); needs to progress.
runners.comment: Promise on Curragh debut but below that level since; needs to progress.

runners.name: Chipsrdown
runners.spotlight: Outran 125-1 odds on her third maiden start at Tipperary last May (7f, yielding to soft) when a distant third of 12; makes handicap debut now but it doesn't look like the handicapper has taken any chances.
runners.comment: Outran 125-1 odds when third on third m'den start; makes h'cap debut now but mark is tough

races.race_name: Easter Maiden Fillies' Stakes (GBB Race)

runners.name: Celestra
runners.spotlight: Runner-up in a pair of 7f novice events on good/good to firm last July, on the second occasion going down narrowly at Newmarket to one who was second in a Listed race in Dubai in February; should be more to come for leading yard.
runners.comment: Runner-up twice over 7f on turf last July; should be more to come for leading yard

runners.name: Thankfully
runners.spotlight: St Mark's Basilica filly who cost 260,000gns as a yearling; shaped well when third of seven in Sandown maiden (7f, soft) on debut, with subsequent Prix Marcel Boussac fourth Esna only narrowly ahead of her in second; on the back of that she went off at 4-5 for a Newbury novice (1m, soft) in October but faded into fifth (Lovers Leap sixth); evidently well regarded and given another chance to confirm that debut promise.
runners.comment: Third on debut; beaten at 5-6 since but evidently well regarded and given another chance
runners.owner: Mrs Tabor, Mrs Mv Magnier, Mrs Shanahan

runners.name: Aurelune
runners.spotlight: Two outings on Tapeta this year, beaten just under 10l when third over 8.6f at Wolverhampton latterly; improvement needed now switching to turf.
runners.comment: Modest form in two AW outings this year

runners.name: Sierra Sue
runners.spotlight: £42,000 Starman half-sister to winners Roman Spring (5f/6f including AW; RPR 76) and Only In Jakarta (7f AW; 76); dam unraced half-sister to 5f Listed winner Swiss Lake (dam of sprint Group 3 winners Swiss Spirit and Swiss Diva); arguably a bit of a surprise to see her start over an extended 1m given the speed in her pedigree but still worth a market check.
runners.comment: Quite a speedy pedigree to start out over this trip but still needs a betting check

runners.name: Lillie Margot
runners.spotlight: Nice debut when chasing home a couple of useful sorts over 1m at Haydock (good) last September; only seventh when favourite at Leicester (1m again) the following month but she retains potential on her return.
runners.comment: Promise on first of two 1m starts last year; retains potential on return

runners.name: Lovers Leap
runners.spotlight: 450,000gns yearling; showed some promise with fourth of nine on 7f Wolverhampton debut in September; perhaps soft ground didn't suit when sixth (Thankfully fifth) of eight at Newbury (1m) later in the autumn; cheekpieces are reached for on reappearance.
runners.comment: Promise on AW debut but only sixth on soft turf next time; cheekpieces added on return

runners.name: Wheres Veneker
runners.spotlight: 50-1 and finished down the field on 7f Newcastle debut five weeks ago; significant improvement needed now switching to turf.
runners.comment: 50-1 and finished down the field on 7f Newcastle debut five weeks ago

runners.name: Wadacre Geisha
runners.spotlight: 25-1 and no short-term promise on 9.4f Wolverhampton debut last month.
runners.comment: 25-1 and no short-term promise on 9.4f Wolverhampton debut last month

runners.name: Yorkies Dream
runners.spotlight: Finished well for third of five on 7f Redcar debut (good to soft) last October but form is nothing specials and she was well beaten at Bath later in the month.
runners.comment: Modest third on Redcar debut and well held at Bath next time
runners.owner: T Cottier, On Top Racing, Amity Fin

races.race_name: Ire-Incentive, It Pays To Buy Irish EBF Fillies' Restricted Novice Stakes (GBB/IRE Inc') (Div I)

runners.name: Donna Rumma
runners.spotlight: This February foal cost £31,000 as a yearling and is the first foal of a 6f AW winner (RPR 89); stable quick to get among the juvenile winners this year and market confidence would look very significant.
runners.comment: Market confidence significant for a stable quick to get among 2yo winners this year

runners.name: Baila Morena
runners.spotlight: The youngest in the line-up as an April foal; 3,000gns half-sister to 5f winner Bobbi Rosa (RPR 88); dam unraced half-sister to winners Soldier's Call (5f 2yo Group 2); probably best watched.
runners.comment: The youngest in the line-up and probably best watched
runners.owner: T&t Racing Ltd And Keith West

runners.name: Bluestone Lady
runners.spotlight: February foal; 18,000euros half-sister to winners Dark Rosa (6f turf 2yo/8.6f AW; RPR 77) and Tiger Mylk (1m 2yo; 68); Karl Burke won this with one in the same colours last year; likely type
runners.comment: Likely type for top yard which won this with one in the Nick Bradley silks last year

runners.name: Lairy Mary
runners.spotlight: This daughter of Tasleet is a January foal and the first out of a mare who was placed over 1m2f on AW (RPR 76); others make greater paper appeal.
runners.comment: Tasleet filly who is best watched on debut unless betting strongly suggests otherwise

runners.name: Lauralynn
runners.spotlight: January foal; half-sister to Swedish 6f 2yo/1m winner Black Bear; dam sprint maiden (RPR 67); others make more obvious appeal on breeding but the yard did have a smart 2yo last year.
runners.comment: Others make more appeal on breeding but the yard had a smart 2yo last year

runners.name: May Bee In Profit
runners.spotlight: March foal; 8,000gns half-sister to winners J J Stingleton (1m AW 2yo; RPR 63) and Longlife Elisir (Italian 6f/7f including 2yo); dam 7.4f 2yo winner (71); the mount of David Allan from the yard's three runners but the draw could have been kinder.
runners.comment: The mount of David Allan from yard's three runners but the draw could have been kinder

runners.name: Princesse d'Orange
runners.spotlight: March foal; half-sister to 6f winner Diligently (including 2yo; RPR 91); dam was a 6f winner and is a half-sister to winners Audience (1m Group 1), Esquire (7f Group 3) and Dark Lady (6f 2yo Group 3); makes plenty of paper appeal and very interesting to see what the betting makes of her.
runners.comment: Makes plenty of paper appeal for good stable

runners.name: Tango Hotel
runners.spotlight: February foal by Mohaather and the second foal of a 6f winner (including 2yo; RPR 90); first juvenile runner of the season for George Scott; has the inside stall and would very much enter the reckoning if the betting suggests she's fancied.
runners.comment: Has the inside stall and would enter reckoning if betting vibes are strong

runners.name: Amantha
runners.spotlight: February foal by Mohaather; 16,000euros foal; dam 6f-1m maiden in UAE (RPR 64), half-sister to US 1m Grade 3 winner Carressa, out of US 1m/8.5f stakes winner; stable doesn't have many 2yos go in first time; best watched unless the betting hints otherwise
runners.comment: Yard doesn't have many 2yos go in first time; best watched unless betting hints otherwise

runners.name: Fern Clyde
runners.spotlight: January foal; 22,000gns half-sister to winners Song Of The Clyde (6f 2yo; RPR 96) and Flicka's Girl (5f/6f AW inc 2yo; 75); dam 5f AW 2yo winner (57); one of three newcomers for a yard which won this in 2019 (second time up) and sent out the useful Argentine Tango to finish second on debut last year.
runners.comment: One of three newcomers from the stable and the betting should provide more clues

runners.name: The Can Can Queen
runners.spotlight: This February foal is a half-sister to winners Grand Libya (French 6.5f-1m AW; RPR 87) and Cancan In The Rain (7f/1m including 2yo/AW; 84); dam French 1m1f winner, half-sister to winners Starboard (1m2f Group 3) and Predilection (Listed-placed 1m); market check needed for a stable which has tasted success in this race in recent years.
runners.comment: Betting check needed for a stable which has tasted success in this race in recent years

races.race_name: Ire-Incentive, It Pays To Buy Irish EBF Fillies' Restricted Novice Stakes (GBB/IRE Inc') (Div II)

runners.name: Bayside
runners.spotlight: Made an encouraging start to her career when running on from the rear to snatch third at Bath (5f, good) ten days ago; sure to improve.
runners.comment: Looks a sure-fire improver after promising debut third at Bath ten days ago
runners.owner: Middleham Park Racing Civ & B R Millman

runners.name: Bellatina
runners.spotlight: February foal; picked up for only £10,000 as a yearling but is a half-sister to smart 5f winner Adrestia (including 2yo; RPR 106); the draw could have been kinder but she still needs monitoring in the betting.
runners.comment: This Belardo filly is drawn widest but makes plenty of appeal on breeding

runners.name: Celestial Noir
runners.spotlight: April foal; 19,000gns  half-sister to winners Bellagio Man (6f AW/7f turf; RPR 91), Je Ne Sais Quoi (5f/6f including 2yo; 87) and Ridicule (Qatari 6f 2yo); one of two from her yard and of four for her owner; the betting should help guide to expectations.
runners.comment: One of two from her yard and of four for her owner; the betting should help guide

runners.name: Happy Humpo
runners.spotlight: April foal who went through the ring for just 1,000gns last autumn but is out of a mare who was a 6f 2yo winner (RPR 81); the market should provide more clues.
runners.comment: The market should provide clues with this newcomer

runners.name: Lady Blanche
runners.spotlight: January foal; £18,000 daughter of Persian Force; dam unplaced 7f/1m2f (RPR 69), half-sister to three winners, including useful  Enthrall (French 1m/1m1f); with a top yard so market support would look significant.
runners.comment: Represents top connections so market support would look significant.

runners.name: Lady Dublin
runners.spotlight: March foal; 8,000gns yearling; dam unraced half-sister to 8.6f 2yo winner Beau Nash; probably best watched.
runners.comment: Probably best watched on debut

runners.name: Showmethebubbles
runners.spotlight: February foal; £17,000 sister to Swedish 6f winner Showcasis Oasis; dam 5f 2yo winner (RPR 81); another in the Nick Bradley silks; the percentage call is to look elsewhere.
runners.comment: Yard not a regular source of first-time-out success

runners.name: Sunrise At Dawn
runners.spotlight: March foal; dam unraced sister to US 1m Grade 3 winner Lammas; could only consider if backed.
runners.comment: Probably best to look elsewhere unless the betting strongly hints otherwise

runners.name: Suzie May
runners.spotlight: March foal who cost 2,000gns as a yearling; half-sister to 6f winner Grey Horizon (including 2yo/AW; RPR 75); stable not really known for 2yo success.
runners.comment: Stable not really known for 2yo success

runners.name: Pageant Girl
runners.spotlight: March foal; £31,000 Acclamation filly and the second foal of a 5f winner (including 2yo; RPR 94); has a handy draw and hails from an excellent stable so there are reasons to be positive.
runners.comment: Handily drawn and hails from excellent stable so reasons to be positive

runners.name: Stripes Of Glory
runners.spotlight: March foal; dam Listed-placed 5f 2yo winner (RPR 93) from a speedy family; worth a second look in the betting for a stable which has won this in recent years.
runners.comment: Worth a second look in the betting for a stable which has won this in recent years.

races.race_name: Beverley Beaker Handicap

runners.name: Sports Coach
runners.spotlight: Built on his Southwell reappearance second when seeing off five rivals back there (5f) three weeks later; respectable fifth of 12 in a competitive race at Newcastle (5f) since; while his peak RPRs have been achieved on AW he was placed at Redcar last summer and it's too soon to suggest he won't prove fully effective on the grass; could have fitness edge over some.
runners.comment: Shouldn't be too far away if he can carry good recent AW form back to the grass

runners.name: Venture Capital
runners.spotlight: Below par in three AW outings this year but is well treated on last year's turf exploits if first-time blinkers spark him back to life.
runners.comment: Well treated on last year's turf exploits if benefiting from first-time blinkers

runners.name: Tiriac
runners.spotlight: Reacted well to a belated switch to AW at the end of 2025, winning twice over 5f at Newcastle; behind Sports Coach back there last month but it was his first outing for 104 days so he could be sharper now.
runners.comment: Two AW wins in late 2025; might have needed a recent run at Newcastle after 104 days off

runners.name: Corolla Point
runners.spotlight: Purchased for 60,000gns from Charlie Appleby last July and looked very useful when landing 5f handicaps at Haydock (good) and Hamilton (soft) for Ed Bethell in the autumn; only had five career starts so it'll be a surprise if there isn't more to come this year; leading claims with the stable among the winners.
runners.comment: Won two 5f handicaps for new yard last autumn and should be more to come this year

runners.name: Duran
runners.spotlight: Pushed the reopposing Smart Vision close at Doncaster last autumn; started last season well (including second in this race on reappearance) and should be on the premises if resuming in similar form.
runners.comment: Started 2025 well (including second in this on return) and claims if resuming in same form
runners.owner: D Caslin, P Wilcock & M Cantillon

runners.name: Smart Vision
runners.spotlight: Better than ever last autumn, winning 5f handicaps at Leicester (good to soft) and Doncaster (heavy); creditable fourth (Duran second) in this race on his 2025 reappearance; likely to be competitive from a handy draw.
runners.comment: Pair of 5f wins last autumn; fourth in this on 2025 reappearance; handy draw; claims
runners.owner: J E Abbey And New Vision Bloodstock

runners.name: I'm Next
runners.spotlight: Prominent racer who notched two 5f wins last year, including a defeat of Sunday's Musselburgh scorer Montezuma at Musselburgh (5f, good to soft) in September; not so good on final two starts but he's still relatively lightly raced as a sprinter and appeals as one who could resume his progression at some point in 2026.
runners.comment: Some of 2025 reads well appeals as one who may have more to offer at some point this year

runners.name: Air Force One
runners.spotlight: Good form in York sprints last summer; no more than respectable efforts in two AW outings this winter but feasibly treated on those York efforts if a return to turf sparks a revival.
runners.comment: Not at best in two AW runs this winter but claims on last summer's York form

runners.name: Acrisius
runners.spotlight: Successful over 5f at Chester (5f, good) last July; also went close on his 2025 reappearance which provides hope that he may be ready to roll after seven months off; drawn widest.
runners.comment: 5f Chester win last year; also went close on 2025 reappearance so could be ready to roll

runners.name: Squealer
runners.spotlight: Lost his way last autumn but returns off 2lb lower than when winning at York (5f, good) under regular rider Rowan Scott last summer so he has plenty of handicapping scope if reviving after six months off; yard also runs Duran.
runners.comment: Below par last autumn but is on a good mark if reviving after six months off

races.race_name: 140th Year Of The Watt Memorial Handicap (GBBPlus Race)

runners.name: Anniversary
runners.spotlight: 4yo who has had only seven races; can race freely but there's still a feeling he could have a bigger performance in him and he shaped quite well under a change of tactics (went from the front) when 4l fifth of nine in the Queen's Prize at Kempton (2m, AW) 18 days ago.
runners.comment: Races freely but still relatively unexposed and latest Kempton fifth came in a good race

runners.name: Sax Appeal
runners.spotlight: Prominent racer who posted four wins in the second half of last year, including a C&D defeat of Charging Thunder on good; suffered a heavy defeat on AW last time but it was in a strong Good Friday race at Newcastle and Charging Thunder was once again behind him; a bold show seems likely in these much calmer waters back on turf.
runners.comment: C&D winner; latest heavy AW defeat came in strong race; this turf race is easier; player

runners.name: Wahraan
runners.spotlight: Very useful in this sphere at his peak but it's doubtful he still retains that level of ability, finishing last of six in 2m AW handicap at Southwell back on the Flat last month.
runners.comment: Last of six in 2m AW handicap back on Flat last month and others are preferred

runners.name: Charging Thunder
runners.spotlight: Second to Sax Appeal over C&D and successful over 1m6f at Wolverhampton (both off 2lb higher than today) last July; it's hard to be enthused by his more recent efforts, including well held at Newcastle on Good Friday, but this is a much less competitive scenario.
runners.comment: Struggled on AW recently but this is easier and he's well treated on last summer's form

runners.name: Elysian Flame
runners.spotlight: Developed into a pretty smart staying handicapper on the Flat for Mick and David Easterby in 2021, finishing third in 2m4f handicaps at Royal Ascot and Glorious Goodwood; respectable fifth of 32 in the Cesarewitch at Newmarket (2m2f) in October of that year but not seen again until fourth of six at Ayr (1m5f, good to soft) last September; there was enough in that to suggest he retains ability but he's been absent for another seven months and isn't the easiest to assess.
runners.comment: Smart in 2024 but last autumn's well-held fourth at Ayr was first run since

races.race_name: Diony Dash Handicap

runners.name: King Of Chaos
runners.spotlight: Won 7f Lingfield (AW) maiden on his third start for Dylan Cunha but hasn't reproduced that in two subsequent AW handicaps; needs to resume his progress back on turf.
runners.comment: AW maiden win in February but held in two AW handicaps since; others more obvious

runners.name: Willowinghurn
runners.spotlight: Second success when making all from Lyrical Song in 7f Musselburgh handicap (soft) last October but well beaten in both subsequent starts and Danny Tudhope, who was on board at Musselburgh, partners stablemate Sandy Craic.
runners.comment: Saw off Lyrical Song to win in October but well held final two runs; Tudhope on stablemate

runners.name: Solar Cooper
runners.spotlight: Failed to progress from his opening third in three subsequent starts for Brian Ellison last year, despite being 7-4 favourite for handicap debut at Thirsk (6f, good) in August when last seen; returns from nearly nine months off, under the care of a new trainer.
runners.comment: Failed to progress last year, including sixth when favourite for handicap debut; new yard

runners.name: Indefensible
runners.spotlight: Left debut behind when promising third of 13 at Newcastle (6f) last August; went off favourite for a Wolverhampton novice (6f again) the following month but stopped quickly and finished sixth; absent for seven months ahead of this handicap and turf debut.
runners.comment: Promise on second of three AW starts last year; off 211 days ahead of this handicap debut

runners.name: Lady Hornblower
runners.spotlight: Won a fast-ground C&D novice last August and bumped into subsequent Listed runner-up Caim when second under a penalty back here (soft) in September; never involved in a big-field sales race at Newmarket final start but those C&D performances provide hope that she could go well now handicapping after a six-month break.
runners.comment: First then second in maiden/novice over C&D last year; respected handicap newcomer

runners.name: Top Lad
runners.spotlight: Well held on AW in November but did show some promise at up to on turf prior to that; off for five months ahead of this handicap debut, with the betting perhaps the best guide to expectations.
runners.comment: Well held on AW last November but showed promise on turf before that; handicap debut

runners.name: Cosmic Man
runners.spotlight: Three outings over 6f last year, showing promise when keeping on for close fourth at Carlisle (good) on second start; blew the start at Ayr (soft) four weeks later, so can have that fourth of eight marked up slightly; subsequently gelded; potential improver and one of the more likely types, particularly if backed.
runners.comment: Promise in three runs last year; potential improver in handicaps after gelding operation

runners.name: Lyrical Song
runners.spotlight: Ended 2025 with a second to the reopposing Willowinghurn on 7f Musselburgh handicap debut (soft); may have more to offer in 2026 but the betting is perhaps the best guide as to whether he's likely to be fully primed for this first outing in 196 days (has been gelded).
runners.comment: Second to Willowinghurn on 7f handicap debut last autumn; gelded and off 196 days

runners.name: Ubetterseethis
runners.spotlight: Kept busy as a 2yo with 12 runs, winning a 7f Chester seller (good to firm); left the impression a stiff 1m at Pontefract (soft) just stretched him on final start; this slightly shorter trip could therefore suit on reappearance but he has a much more exposed profile than most; has been gelded.
runners.comment: Had 12 starts at 2 so he's much more exposed than most; gelded ahead more return

runners.name: Sandy Craic
runners.spotlight: Advanced her RPR with each start last summer, finishing second of 16 in a restricted novice at York (7f, good) in July; while that York form hasn't really worked out, she still brings unexposed potential to this handicap debut after nearly nine months off; check the betting.
runners.comment: Off since July but progressed with each start last year; check betting on handicap debut

races.race_name: Spillers Powering Your Winners Fillies' Handicap

runners.name: Holly Mist
runners.spotlight: Placed in all four starts, including when keeping-on third on her handicap debut at Southwell (1m, AW) ten days ago; same mark and should be fine returned to turf; fitness is guaranteed and she's a leading contender.
runners.comment: Good handicap debut when third (1m, AW) ten days ago; unexposed and might be the answer

runners.name: Glorious Kitty
runners.spotlight: 12-race maiden but she was in good form when runner-up twice over this sort of trip in October, notably when beaten a neck at Nottingham (good); step up to 1m2f backfired when favourite at Chelmsford (AW) the following month but she's back down in distance for this return; respected.
runners.comment: Second twice over this sort of trip in October; latest start (1m2f) can be excused; chance

runners.name: Quirke On Parole
runners.spotlight: Justified short odds in 1m2f handicap here (good to firm) in June and ran well when second in similar race here next time; didn't see out final two starts last year, so maybe this drop back in trip can assist on her reappearance.
runners.comment: Three good 1m2f runs in 2025 included win here; back in trip for return but not ruled out
runners.owner: A Quirke & Mrs G B Walford

runners.name: Fire Eyes
runners.spotlight: Won three turf handicaps (1m/7f) in 2025, all in a tongue-tie and the latest with cheekpieces on for the first time; no headgear for either start last month (probably didn't stay 1m2f last time) and the accessories are missing again; back on her last winning mark, though.
runners.comment: All three 2025 wins featured headgear; no aids again, but fit and on last winning mark

runners.name: Blufferonthebus
runners.spotlight: Course winner (7.5f, soft) in 2024 who won two 7f handicaps (soft/AW) last November; blew the start when beating one home (same mark) the following month and absent since; stays this trip, though, and she's not readily dismissed.
runners.comment: Won two 7f handicaps in November; stays this trip and she's not readily dismissed

runners.name: Princess Niyla
runners.spotlight: Dual winner here (1m2f) including when she beat Dandy's Angel on good ground last May; 0-7 subsequently but she went close at Redcar in August and last week's pipe-opener at Pontefract (raced freely) should help; first go at this sort of trip since second of six at Doncaster in June 2024 and she could go well.
runners.comment: Dual 1m2f course winner who ran well at 1m when last tried nearly 2 years ago; respected

runners.name: Dandy's Angel
runners.spotlight: Six-time course winner (all 1m2f) who goes on any ground and finished second in this on her first start of 2023; 0-10 since her last win in July, however, and she was 50-1 when suffering big defeat on her reappearance in this race last year; nothing wrong with this mark if she is ready to roll.
runners.comment: Six-time course winner (1m2f); goes on any ground; nothing wrong with this mark if ready

runners.name: Wondrous Light
runners.spotlight: Scored at Lingfield (11.5f, good to soft) on her handicap debut in June and well backed when a fair fifth of 14 over this sort of trip at Newmarket (good) in October; would likely appreciate any rain and she's back on her winning mark for this reappearance.
runners.comment: 11.5f winner on handicap debut in June; back on that winning mark for this return

runners.name: Catton Lady
runners.spotlight: Kept on well to beat Blufferonthebus at Carlisle (her only other win also came there) in June; good effort when second of 11 at Thirsk (also 1m, soft) in September when last seen; also ran well on her return in 2024 but could beat only Dandy's Angel when making her comeback in this race last year; any rain would help and market may guide on this reappearance.
runners.comment: Dual Carlisle winner who ran well at Thirsk in September when last seen; market may help
runners.owner: W H & Mrs J A Tinning & Mrs C Wallis

runners.name: Special Breeze
runners.spotlight: Appeared to show improved form when fourth of five at Southwell (1m, AW) in September on her final qualifying run; assessor probably hasn't left much to chance but she's the least exposed of these and may be able to have a say on this handicap debut.
runners.comment: Appeared to improve on last qualifying run and she's unexposed on this handicap debut
runners.owner: Michael H Sly Dr T Davies Mrs Pam Sly

races.race_name: Racing Again Next Thursday Handicap

runners.name: Havachoc
runners.spotlight: Landed a hat-trick of 1m4f AW wins across January/February and has continued to run respectably, including when third over 1m6f at Wolverhampton a fortnight ago; 0-6 record on turf is a little disconcerting, but otherwise respected.
runners.comment: 1m4f AW hat-trick earlier this year; candidate if he can produce his best on turf

runners.name: Arth's Gold
runners.spotlight: 0-12 but he's threatening to win a race and was only just picked off when tackling 1m4f for the first time at Southwell (AW) ten days ago; slight question to answer on just this second turf start but otherwise a player from the same mark.
runners.comment: Only just picked off over 1m4f on AW ten days ago; player if transferring that to turf

runners.name: Freddy Robinson
runners.spotlight: 1-23 on the Flat but the win came at this track (1m2f, good to soft) in June 2024; plenty of other good efforts here, including over this trip, and he was a winner over hurdles in December; should benefit from his reappearance in early March; can go well.
runners.comment: Won here (1m2f) in 2024; has run well at this track on other occasions and stays this trip

runners.name: Highfield Comrade
runners.spotlight: Record of 41333 since switched to AW, the win in a Southwell classified over 1m3f in January; respectable third on turf at Thirsk (1m4f, good to firm) in August, however, and he won't lack for fitness back on grass.
runners.comment: Third on turf in August and improved on AW this year; won't lack for fitness; respected

runners.name: Irv
runners.spotlight: Course winner in 2022 who last won at Pontefract (also 1m2f, good to firm) in June 2024; 50-1 when beaten 10l on his comeback in a Wolverhampton classified (1m4f, AW) in January; others look safer.
runners.comment: 50-1 when beaten 10l on his return in Wolverhampton classified (1m4f, AW) in January

runners.name: Nymphaea
runners.spotlight: 0-9 but she wasn't far away at Newcastle (1m4f) in February and went pretty close at Southwell (1m6f) the following month; disappointed in Lingfield classified (also AW) three weeks ago but she's not entirely ruled out in this first turf handicap.
runners.comment: Disappointed latest (AW) but she's not ruled out in her first handicap on turf

runners.name: Inspiring Speeches
runners.spotlight: Back-to-back wins last summer came over 1m2f here (soft) and when narrowly making all at Catterick (1m4f, good); both wins came under Tom Kiely-Marshall but they also featured cheekpieces, which are missing for this comeback; mark is fine if he is ready.
runners.comment: Two summer wins came wearing cheekpeices (1m4f and here over 1m2f); no headgear this time

runners.name: Sugarpiehoneybunch
runners.spotlight: Won at Hamilton (1m4f) in June when returning from a 227-day break and beaten less than 3l when fourth to Sea Master there (1m5f, also good) next time; not in the same form subsequently but might do better back from her winter break.
runners.comment: Won at Hamilton in June after a longer break than this; may go well on this comeback

runners.name: Sweet Cicely
runners.spotlight: 18-race maiden who turned in one of her better efforts when beaten just 1l here (1m2f; cheekpieces, not worn today) on stable debut in June; last two starts have seen heavy defeats, however.
runners.comment: 18-race maiden; last two starts have seen heavy defeats

runners.name: Muhib
runners.spotlight: Yet to score in 19 attempts but not disgraced on last three starts, beaten just over 2l at Catterick (1m4f, good) under Warren Fentiman one week ago; stiff 1m4f is not certain to suit but he might be on the premises.
runners.comment: Yet to win in 19 attempts but not disgraced on last three starts; might be on the premises

runners.name: Sea Master
runners.spotlight: Consistent first year with Ruth Carr included solid second over C&D (good to firm) and a win over 1m5f at Hamilton (good) in July; form tailed off markedly on final two starts of 2025 but no surprise if he resumes in better form following a break.
runners.comment: Solid 2nd over C&D and won Hamilton h'cap in July; no surprise if he goes well on return

runners.name: Sisterandbrother
runners.spotlight: Exposed 8yo but he won for the sixth time when landing 1m4f handicap at Kempton (AW; first-time cheekpieces) on his penultimate start for Shaun Harris; 4lb higher for this stable debut (same owner) and the cheekpieces are missing again.
runners.comment: Won January AW h'cap (1m4f) in cheekpieces; those aids are missing for this stable debut

races.race_name: Celebrate With Sponsorship At Southwell Racecourse EBF Novice Stakes (GBB Race)

runners.name: Pershaada
runners.spotlight: Closely related to 6f 2yo winner Teej A (RPR 87); went off odds-on when third of eight at Wolverhampton (5f, AW) on her debut nine days ago, having had to briefly wait for a run at the top of the straight; open to improvement.
runners.comment: Went off odds-on when third on her debut at Wolverhampton; open to improvement; player

runners.name: Love A Giggle
runners.spotlight: 38,000euros foal, 130,000gns yearling; February foal; by Kodiac; first foal; dam 5f 2yo winner (RPR 82), half-sister to winners Futuro Anteriore (useful Italian 5f-11.5f including 2yo) and Elusive Beauty (7f Listed), out of 1m1f winner; interesting newcomer for a leading stable.
runners.comment: 38,000euros foal, 130,000gns yearling; by Kodiac; dam 5f 2yo winner; interesting newcomer

runners.name: Bettabebougie
runners.spotlight: April foal; Ubettabelieveit half-sister to Czech 1m1f winner Stylhara; dam unraced sister to winners Humoresque (10.5f Group 3) and Mighty (Group-placed 1m2f), half-sister to 5f Group 2 winner Danehurst; watch the betting on her debut.
runners.comment: April foal; Ubettabelieveit half-sister to Czech 1m1f winner Stylhara; watch the betting

runners.name: Havana Laff
runners.spotlight: £80,000 yearling; March foal; Havana Grey half-sister to winners Cressida Wildes (5f including AW; RPR 84) and Onigiri (6f 2yo; 76); dam 5f 2yo winner (87), half-sister to useful winners Compton Park (6f/7f) and Dance Company (7f), out of 5f 2yo/6f winner; speedy pedigree and she's of interest on her debut.
runners.comment: Half-sister to winners Cressida Wildes (5f) and Onigiri (6f); dam 5f 2yo winner; contender
runners.owner: Upton House Stud & Whitsbury Manor Stud

races.race_name: Country & Western Night 25th April Handicap

runners.name: Invitorio
runners.spotlight: Sole win from 18 starts came in a novice at Redcar (6f, soft) in October 2024; well beaten in his first starts since returning from a break but he came back to form from a career-low mark when second of 12 at Catterick (5f, good) a week ago; that was his first try over 5f and he's contender.
runners.comment: 1-18 but he came back to form when runner-up on turf at Catterick a week ago; contender
runners.owner: Grange Park Racing Xviii And Ruth Carr

runners.name: Anglesey Lad
runners.spotlight: 19-race maiden but he did have some solid placed efforts on turf at Brighton last year; however, he beat only one rival home at Wolverhampton (5f, AW) when last seen and he may need this first outing for 309 days.
runners.comment: 0-19 but had some solid placed efforts on turf last year; may need this reappearance

runners.name: Irish Dancer
runners.spotlight: Dual C&D winner last year and he's been runner-up here in two starts since returning from a 167-day break, most recently behind a progressive rival 18 days ago; another good run looks likely.
runners.comment: Dual C&D winner and he's been runner-up in two starts since returning from a break; claims

runners.name: Poetic Jack
runners.spotlight: Runner-up in a classified event at Chelmsford (6f, AW) in January but he failed to back that up next time; caught inside the final furlong before finishing fourth of ten there 48 days ago and now attempts 5f for just the second time; visor applied.
runners.comment: Caught inside the final furlong over 6f at Chelmsford last time; visor is now applied

runners.name: Desert Master
runners.spotlight: Two of his three wins have been over C&D and he was second of eight here in a classified event in February; below his best in two starts since but he may be capable of bouncing back.
runners.comment: Dual C&D winner who was second here in February; below his best in two starts since
runners.owner: John And Tony Jarvis And Partner

runners.name: Fortunate Star
runners.spotlight: Won classified events over C&D in February and at Newcastle in March; placed in handicap company on both starts since, latest when a close third of 13 here 13 days; good claims once again.
runners.comment: Placed in two handicaps since winning two classified events (one over C&D); good claims

runners.name: Bang On The Bell
runners.spotlight: Won at Windsor last April off a mark 12lb higher than today's and he took a step back in the right direction when a close-up third at Nottingham (5f) a week ago; claims if building on that now returned to the AW.
runners.comment: 12lb below his last winning mark; back to form at Nottingham a week ago; back on the AW

runners.name: Hi Hoh Tonto
runners.spotlight: 19-race maiden who was well beaten in four starts for Adrian Wintle last term; didn't shape badly when sixth at Chelmsford on his first start for this stable last month having been off for nearly a year; may come on for that.
runners.comment: 0-19 but he didn't shape badly on his yard debut having been off for nearly a year

runners.name: Noble Phoenix
runners.spotlight: Promise in novice/maiden events for Andrew Balding but he's been well beaten in seven handicaps since, including five for this stable; drops to 5f with a transformation needed; best watched.
runners.comment: Well beaten in seven handicaps, including five for this stable; drops to 5f; best watched

runners.name: Alfa Sea Breeze
runners.spotlight: Promise in her maiden/novice runs (5f-7f) and she had a nightmare passage on her handicap debut at Beverley (5f, good to firm) last July; had a wide trip when down the field here over 6f last month on her first start for 252 days; dropped back in trip with a tongue-tie applied and she may well take a step forward from that effort.
runners.comment: Promise in maiden/novice runs and excuses in two handicaps since; tongue-tie now added
runners.owner: Alfa Site Services Ltd & Syps (Uk) Ltd

races.race_name: Book The Bistro In Sherwood Restaurant Handicap

runners.name: Fashionelle
runners.spotlight: 0-12 in Flat handicaps but she was placed here in November on two occasions (including C&D); behind Shipshape at Wolverhampton (1m6f) on his first start for 131 days but the returning cheekpieces should be a positive.
runners.comment: Placed here in November on two occasions; behind Shipshape at Wolverhampton last time

runners.name: Betelgeuse
runners.spotlight: Showed the benefit of a gelding operation when winning a C&D handicap by 9l in October but he was raised 8lb and was well held on his next start; needs to bounce back following a 166-day break.
runners.comment: Won by 9l over C&D in October but well held on his next start; returns from 166 days off

runners.name: Sycamore Gap
runners.spotlight: Off the mark at the 17th attempt when winning at Newcastle (1m4f, AW) in September 2024 on his final start for Rebecca Menzies; didn't shape badly here over 1m4f on his stable debut last time having been off for 558 days; may build on that over this longer trip.
runners.comment: 1-17; didn't shape badly here over 1m4f on his return from 558 days off; back up in trip

runners.name: Mio Amico
runners.spotlight: Successful under Kyle McHugh at Lingfield (1m4f, AW) in January and Chelmsford (1m6f, AW) last month; ran another good race when second of nine off this mark at Kempton (2m, AW) nine days ago and has solid claims returned to this trip.
runners.comment: Has won twice under Kyle McHugh this year and ran well over 2m at Kempton last time

runners.name: Shipshape
runners.spotlight: 0-7; shaped with encouragement here over 1m4f on his yard debut and backed up that impression when second of ten at Wolverhampton (1m6f, AW) 15 days ago; unexposed over this distance and he's a major contender.
runners.comment: Shaped with encouragement in two starts for this yard, including over this trip last time

runners.name: Provision
runners.spotlight: Showed promise in novice/maiden company when trained by Paul Midgley; struggled on his yard/hurdling debut in December and it was the same story in a handicap at Lingfield (1m4f, AW) three weeks ago; down 3lb.
runners.comment: Promise for Paul Midgley but has struggled in two starts for this stable; dropped 3lb

runners.name: Geordie Mackem
runners.spotlight: Placed in bumpers on his first two starts but he's yet to really threaten in this sphere; dropped 2lb since finishing down the field over C&D on his handicap debut 83 days ago and now has cheekpieces applied.
runners.comment: Down the field on his handicap debut over C&D 83 days ago; dropped 2lb; cheekpieces added
runners.owner: The Cat And The Mag Partnership

runners.name: Moon Chime
runners.spotlight: Dual winner in bumpers and a four-time winner over hurdles; below his best over hurdles last time but he has shown promise in this sphere, including when third of seven in a Ffos Las novice (1m4f) last August, and is interesting on this Flat handicap debut.
runners.comment: Has shown promise in this sphere, including at Ffos Las last August; interesting contender
runners.owner: DL Adams, JA Adams & G McPherson

races.race_name: Come Racing For Free 18th June Novice Stakes (GBB Race)

runners.name: Penny Time
runners.spotlight: Overcame inexperience to cross the line in front on his debut at Kempton (1m) last month; subsequently disqualified after his rider weighed in light but he was impressive when winning over this C&D ten days later, looks a nice prospect and holds good claims under a penalty.
runners.comment: Disqualified on his debut at Kempton but looked a good prospect when winning at Southwell

runners.name: Goldinthesea
runners.spotlight: Built on the encouragement of his debut when winning at Dundalk (1m, AW) in January, coming 3l clear; subsequently left Joseph O'Brien and now makes his stable debut; one to note in the betting.
runners.comment: Got off the mark over 1m at Dundalk on his second start; has since left Joseph O'Brien

runners.name: The Anthony Gover
runners.spotlight: Out of a French/German 1m2f-1m3f winner (including Group 2; RPR 109); showed plenty when third of seven on his debut at Wolverhampton (8.5f, AW) 29 days ago, dropping away late having enjoyed the run of things; open to improvement.
runners.comment: Dictated steady fractions and showed plenty when third on his debut at Wolverhampton

runners.name: Harmonics
runners.spotlight: Second foal out of a French 1m4f winner, herself a half-sister to French 1m4f Group 3 winner Ottery; one to watch in the betting on his debut; powerful yard.
runners.comment: Second foal out of a French 1m4f winner; one to watch in the betting on his debut

runners.name: Heir Of Ambition
runners.spotlight: First foal out of a 5f AW winner (RPR 79), herself a sister to winners Glory Of Rome (French 5f/6f) and Tudor Icon (Greek 7); already gelded and the market should be informative on his debut.
runners.comment: Gelded; first foal out of a 5f AW winner (RPR 79); market should be informative on debut

runners.name: Shadow Boxer
runners.spotlight: 520,000euros yearling; closely related to Group-placed 7f/1m winner Mithras (including 2yo/AW/HK; RPR 108); dam 7f and 1m (Listed) 2yo winner/French 1,000 Guineas runner-up (113), half-sister to useful 1m 2yo and 2m-2m6f hurdle/chase winner Altruism; nice pedigree and he's of interest on his debut, particularly if the market speaks favourably.
runners.comment: 520,000euros yearling; closely related to Group-placed 7f/1m winner Mithras; interesting

runners.name: Sassicaia
runners.spotlight: Half-brother to 1m3f AW winner Kingmont (RPR 71); had plenty to do turning for home but picked up well to make a winning debut at Wolverhampton (8.5f, AW) in December; that bare form isn't overly strong but he's open to plenty of progress, though that may not come until he's tackling slightly longer trips.
runners.comment: Picked up well to make a winning debut at Wolverhampton (8.5f) in December; absent since

runners.name: Brighton View
runners.spotlight: Showed promise when fifth of nine on his debut at Haydock (1m, good to soft) but he wasn't able to build on that when filling that same position behind a nice prospect at Kempton (1m, AW) 182 days ago; off since.
runners.comment: Failed to build on his debut when fifth at Kempton in October; has been off since

runners.name: Baron Wagstaff
runners.spotlight: Showed some ability behind Penny Time (subsequently disqualified) at Kempton (1m, AW) on his debut but he was well beaten at Bath (1m2f, good) ten days ago; drops back to 1m on this return to the AW; may be one for handicaps.
runners.comment: Showed some ability on his debut but he was well beaten over 1m2f at Bath last time

runners.name: Niffler
runners.spotlight: Beaten 13l when eighth of ten here over 7f on his debut ten days ago; now steps up to 1m but he's likely one for further down the line.
runners.comment: Beaten 13l when eighth of ten here over 7f on his debut; likely one for later on

runners.name: Ziggy's Avenger
runners.spotlight: Closely related to Qatari 12.4f winner Beat The Clock; beat only one rival home on his debut at Nottingham (8.5f, soft; 28-1) in October and he now switches to the AW having been off since; much better needed.
runners.comment: Beat only one rival home on his debut at Nottingham in October; switches to the AW
runners.owner: Middleham Park Racing & Harry Kassap Llc

runners.name: Avispado
runners.spotlight: Showed little on his debut at Doncaster (6f; hooded) in November but that came on testing ground; subsequently gelded and the hood is left off now that he steps up to 1m on his first start since; should be capable of better.
runners.comment: Showed little on his debut at Doncaster (6f, heavy); gelded since and steps up to 1m

races.race_name: Conference & Events At Southwell Racecourse Handicap

runners.name: Rainwater
runners.spotlight: Two wins from 33 starts; runner-up over this C&D in January and he shaped as if still in form when eighth of 22 at Doncaster (1m, good to soft) 17 days ago, weakening late; returns to the AW with solid claims.
runners.comment: Runner-up over C&D in January; weakened late over 1m at Doncaster latest; solid claims

runners.name: Enpassant
runners.spotlight: 2lb lower than for his win at Newcastle in September; below his best in two starts over 1m since finishing fourth over this C&D in February but he should be happier back at 7f.
runners.comment: Below his best over 1m on his last two starts but should be happier back at this trip

runners.name: Orbital Chime
runners.spotlight: Course winner over 6f in November when trained by Katie Scott; didn't quite get home when third at Wolverhampton (8.5f, AW) earlier in the month, and ran well when second of 14 at Thirsk (7f, good) five days ago; may be able to go one better.
runners.comment: Course winner in November over 6f; ran well over this trip at Thirsk five days ago

runners.name: Aspire To Glory
runners.spotlight: Won four handicaps in 2025, including at Chelmsford (7f, AW) on his final start for Alice Haynes; failed to beat a rival home here over 6f on his stable debut but he may come on from that first start for 205 days.
runners.comment: Failed to beat a rival home here over 6f on his stable debut but may come on for that

runners.name: Rusheen Boy
runners.spotlight: Gained his third course win when making all over C&D 83 days ago, clinging on at the line; only 1lb higher on his first start since and makes the shortlist.
runners.comment: Gained his third course win when making all over C&D 83 days ago; only 1lb higher here

runners.name: Call Glory
runners.spotlight: Dual course winner, most recently in a C&D classified event in January; bounced back from a lesser effort when a close-up third here 18 days ago and there's no reason why he can't go well again.
runners.comment: Dual course winner; bounced back from a lesser effort when running well here last time

runners.name: Dancing With Drums
runners.spotlight: 2-2 in handicaps for Martin Dunne, winning here and at Lingfield (both over 6f); didn't get the best of passages at Wolverhampton (6f) on his first start for this yard and looks worth a try over this extra furlong.
runners.comment: 2-2 in handicaps for Martin Dunne; didn't get the best of passages on yard debut; up to 7f

runners.name: Sixteen One
runners.spotlight: 13-race maiden but she went close at Leicester (6f, good to soft) last May when trained by George Boughey; failed to beat a rival home in two starts for Robert Eddery and now makes her debut for another new yard having been off for 244 days; hood applied.
runners.comment: 0-13; failed to beat a rival in two starts for Robert Eddery; hood applied for yard debut

runners.name: Embarked
runners.spotlight: Front-runner who won over C&D in 2024; beaten around 6l when third here in January but he's been well held in two starts since; others preferred.
runners.comment: C&D winner; third here in January but well held in two starts since; others preferred

runners.name: English Lady
runners.spotlight: Two wins from 11 starts, the latest of those wins coming at Kempton (1m, AW) in September off a 3lb lower mark than today's; disappointing there in November and now returns following a 161-day break.
runners.comment: Won over 1m at Kempton in September off 3lb lower but disappointing there when last seen

runners.name: On Key
runners.spotlight: 15-race maiden who didn't run badly at Wolverhampton (6f, AW) two starts ago; however, he was well beaten there nine days ago and isn't sure to be suited by the return to 7f.
runners.comment: 0-15; well beaten at Wolverhampton latest and isn't sure to be suited by the return to 7f

runners.name: Beelzebub
runners.spotlight: Three wins from 32 starts; unlucky not to go closer when fourth of 11 here over 6f last month but he failed to build on that when seventh here ten days ago; steps back up to 7f.
runners.comment: Unlucky not to go closer when fourth here last month but failed to build on that last time

races.race_name: Ladies Day Sunday 16th August Fillies' Handicap

runners.name: Bee My Honey
runners.spotlight: Low-mileage 4yo who may still do better but needs to find such progress, with 2026 form comprising a Chelmsford maiden success that is flanked by lesser efforts at handicap level, latest over C&D.
runners.comment: 2026 form comprises a maiden win that is flanked by lesser efforts at handicap level
runners.owner: The Hot Shots Syndicate & Ms C Musgrave

runners.name: Rogue Citation
runners.spotlight: Both 2yo outings over 6f, finishing third at Carlisle then narrowly justifying favouritism at Southwell; one of her siblings scored over 7f; very similar type to Vencedora and may progress further.
runners.comment: Narrowly justified favouritism at Southwell in the second of her two starts last year

runners.name: Homestrait
runners.spotlight: Not disgraced over C&D on seasonal debut; however, looks reasonably exposed and still needs to prove she can win a handicap, having gained her 2yo successes at maiden/selling level.
runners.comment: Not disgraced over C&D on reappearance but looks reasonably exposed

runners.name: Cool Molly
runners.spotlight: Versatile filly who has gained wins over 5f, 6f and 7f on her way to producing a strike-rate of 3-5 this year; gained the latest success at Lingfield on Good Friday and may not have stopped improving yet.
runners.comment: Won at Lingfield on Good Friday, taking record this year to 3-5; progressive filly

runners.name: Beckon
runners.spotlight: Ran below market expectations at Southwell (handicap debut) three months ago when bidding for a 1m double but shaped as if this drop back to 7f is worth exploring; may do better still and not written off.
runners.comment: Drop back to 7f is worth exploring; may do better still and not written off

runners.name: Ribenska
runners.spotlight: Has come up short in two handicaps but they were on turf and this return to AW may prompt a bigger effort; has form figures of 45221 in this sphere, albeit faced a straightforward task the final time.
runners.comment: Has form figures of 45221 on AW, albeit faced a straightforward task the final time

runners.name: Vencedora
runners.spotlight: Both starts over 6f last September, finishing third at Haydock then beating two rivals at Newcastle (AW); can pick holes in her form but further improvement is possible; likely to stay this extra furlong.
runners.comment: Twice-raced filly; can pick holes in her form but further improvement is possible
runners.owner: Exors Of The Late Sheikh Mohammed Obaid

races.race_name: Summer Socials At Southwell Racecourse Fillies' Handicap

runners.name: Seven Fires
runners.spotlight: 4yo who only made her debut in December and was off the mark on third start when making all to beat ten others in Newcastle maiden (6f) in February; dropped to 5f and had more in hand than the margin suggests when narrowly winning her first handicap here (first-time tongue-tie) last month; handicapper could only put 1lb on her back and she's of firm interest returned to 6f in the hat-trick bid.
runners.comment: On a hat-trick after maiden and handicap wins; unexposed 4yo who's a key player

runners.name: Barefoot Beach
runners.spotlight: Possibly significant she was declared to make her debut at Glorious Goodwood in July (non-runner) and scored at 25-1 when finally given the go ahead at Kempton (6f, Polytrack) in September; positives to take from last month's returning second over that C&D, despite the odds-on reversal, and she carries plenty of potential into this handicap debut.
runners.comment: 1-1 last term and positives to take from recent return; player on handicap debut

runners.name: Fantasy Obsessor
runners.spotlight: 14-1 but she scored on her introduction over C&D in June and defied a penalty in Chelmsford novice (7f, Polytrack) the following month; reverse thrust firmly engaged after that and she didn't beat a rival on her last two starts of 2025; however, she's become attractively weighted and needs a market check ahead of this comeback.
runners.comment: Won her first two but the wheels came off after; handy mark on return; watch the market

runners.name: Zighy
runners.spotlight: Placed on her first four starts and put it all together when taking Wolverhampton novice (6f, Tapeta) in November; may have more to offer this term and she's respected on this reappearance and handicap debut.
runners.comment: Placed on first four starts; won fifth; respected on reappearance/handicap debut
runners.owner: Exors Of The Late Sheikh Mohammed Obaid

runners.name: Moonlight Mirage
runners.spotlight: Built on promising debut (6f, good) in August when landing Wolverhampton novice (Tapeta) three weeks later; back in that form when third in course handicap (also 5f) on final start for George Scott in October and sold for 17,000gns five days later; unexposed, particularly at this trip, and impossible to rule out on this stable/seasonal debut.
runners.comment: Solid third on final start for George Scott; unexposed at this trip; seasonal/stable debut

runners.name: Powdering
runners.spotlight: C&D winner who last scored at Kempton (6f) off this mark in September; just edged out at Lingfield (7f, also Polytrack) on penultimate start and finishing last in a classy Newcastle handicap on Good Friday can be glossed over; not without a chance.
runners.comment: Kempton win and narrow Lingfield second off this mark; a possible

runners.name: Chicory
runners.spotlight: All starts at 6f; won August Newbury maiden (good; form worked out) on her second start and highly tried in two subsequent runs, when down the field in Polytrack Group 3 and Newmarket Listed event; drops in grade for this seasonal/handicap debut and very interesting to see how she goes in the market.
runners.comment: Highly tried after winning debut; drops in grade for seasonal/handicap debut; could figure

runners.name: Addarella
runners.spotlight: Couldn't build on good 6f Wolverhampton record when beaten 10l over that C&D on Friday; has some form here too but she's not easy to recommend after that.
runners.comment: Beaten 10l over her favourite C&D on Friday; not easy to suggest after that

races.race_name: Celebrating 50 Years Of The HK Sevens Handicap (Course A) (Turf)

runners.name: Bingo Babe
runners.spotlight: Put in fairly good front-running shift from stall 12 before weakening into seventh here (6f) in February, but has another double-digit draw for this stable debut and his overall profile remains unappealing.
runners.comment: Has double-digit draw for stable debut and overall profile is not appealing
runners.owner: Charmaine Wong Kam Mei & Chan Fu Ho

runners.name: Taihang Scenery
runners.spotlight: 15-race maiden; outran 34-1 odds when placed over C&D on belated seasonal debut in February, but two lesser efforts (both 6f) have followed and he has the outside draw today.
runners.comment: Placed over C&D in February but two lesser efforts followed and he's drawn wide today

runners.name: Always My Folks
runners.spotlight: Finished last over 6f here last week and is now 0-17, but made the frame over C&D under Zac Purton two starts ago and the champion jockey has maintained the partnership.
runners.comment: Finished last here last week but made the frame over C&D two runs ago; Purton booked again

runners.name: Fortune Warrior
runners.spotlight: Made quite pleasing seasonal/stable debut over 6f on dirt in September, but has been out of form since; drops in grade for this rare 5f run.
runners.comment: Out of form since his quite pleasing stable debut in September; drops in grade today

runners.name: Run Yes Run
runners.spotlight: Disappointing maiden who came last over 6f at Sha Tin on his latest outing in February and is not sure to be suited by this drop back in trip.
runners.comment: Disappointing maiden who came last over 6f at Sha Tin on his latest outing in February
runners.owner: Kong Wing Ming & Edmond Kong Chun Man

runners.name: Sportic Warrior
runners.spotlight: 12-race maiden who has finished well down the field over C&D on both starts this year and is very difficult to enthuse over; tongue-tie refitted.
runners.comment: 12-race maiden who has finished well down the field over C&D on both starts this year
runners.owner: Edmund Wong Yan Ming, Michael Lee To Ming Et Al

runners.name: Happy Boys
runners.spotlight: Reserve; placed over 6f off higher marks for Mark Newnham this season and should be okay back at 5f; will warrant consideration if he gets the chance to make his stable debut today.
runners.comment: Placed twice over 6f this season and has possibilities on stable debut
runners.owner: David Tse Sik Hung, Allison Tse Pui Shan & Q Tse Pui Ching

runners.name: Shine Bright
runners.spotlight: Not disgraced on last two appearances at Sha Tin (7f/6f) but has been placed only once from 17 starts and is unraced over 5f.
runners.comment: Placed only once from 17 starts and unraced over 5f

runners.name: Oriental Surprise
runners.spotlight: Went very close to a breakthrough win over 6f here in February; also effective over 5f and his recent defeat from stall 14 at Sha Tin can be excused, but he has another tough draw here.
runners.comment: Went very close to a breakthrough win in February but has tough draw today

runners.name: Macanese Master
runners.spotlight: Dual C&D winner this season and runner-up on his last two appearances (5f/6f); ideally drawn to attack from the front today and ought to make a bold bid.
runners.comment: Dual C&D winner this season; still in prime form and has ideal draw; good chance

runners.name: Manythanks Forever
runners.spotlight: Reserve; ended 2025 with 7f win at Sha Tin but was safely held off today's mark on both subsequent appearances and makes very limited appeal back at 5f.
runners.comment: Twice safely held off this mark since 7f win in December; opposable back over 5f
runners.owner: Chan Wing Kwong, Patrick Ng Pak Hong Et Al

runners.name: Spicy Spangle
runners.spotlight: Not firing on all cylinders this season, but has been competitive over C&D on all three starts since dropped to this grade this year and continues to slip down the weights; probably won't be far away.
runners.comment: Competitive since dropped to this grade and continues to slip down the weights; considered

runners.name: Country Dancer
runners.spotlight: Won twice (once over C&D) last season, but yet to strike form after three runs this term and managed only tenth when dipping into this grade here (6f) last time.
runners.comment: Yet to strike form this season, managing only tenth when dropped to this grade last time

runners.name: Lucky Generations
runners.spotlight: Dual 6f course winner this season; on a tough mark for this first attempt at 5f but is well drawn and has the services of Joao Moreira.
runners.comment: Two 6f wins this season; on tough mark for first 5f attempt but well drawn

races.race_name: Celebrating 40 Years Of The HKFC10s Handicap (Course A) (Turf)

runners.name: Dragon Sunrise
runners.spotlight: 0-18 but kept on well for close third behind two dead-heaters over C&D last month; only 1lb higher today and Zac Purton keeps the ride; respected.
runners.comment: Nudged up only 1lb for last month's close third and Zac Purton keeps the ride
runners.owner: Sunny Wong Chi Tat & Wong Tsz Wah

runners.name: Wah May Wai Wai
runners.spotlight: Made the frame over C&D in January and remains very well handicapped on early-season form, but his latest run was disappointing and he's now 0-25 in Hong Kong.
runners.comment: Well handicapped on early-season form but disappointed last month; now 0-25 in Hong Kong

runners.name: Smart Trio
runners.spotlight: 3-7 when based in Australia but out of the frame in all 16 local starts; favourable draw not enough to make him of interest.
runners.comment: 3-7 when based in Australia but out of the frame in all 16 local starts
runners.owner: Peter Lee Kwok Wah, Tony Lau Hon Kwan & Kenny Cheng Tsin Ki

runners.name: Courier Magic
runners.spotlight: Placed three consecutive times over 1m on turf (twice here) at start of this season and may not have been ideally suited by dirt when below that form on last three appearances; drawn wide for this turf return but has slipped to a tempting mark; interesting.
runners.comment: Placed three times on turf this season and returns to this surface on a tempting mark

runners.name: Power Summit
runners.spotlight: Nine-race maiden who was soundly beaten when upped to 7f at Sha Tin last month (wore blinkers); upped in trip again and has cheekpieces refitted here; not the most obvious answer.
runners.comment: Nine-race maiden who was soundly beaten when upped to 7f last month; up in trip again here

runners.name: Perfecto Moments
runners.spotlight: 17-race maiden; placed twice over C&D this season and was close fourth (just behind Dragon Sunrise) here last month, despite a wide trip from stall 10; drawn wide again but still considered.
runners.comment: Close fourth here last month, despite a wide trip; considered, despite another tough draw

runners.name: Exceed The Wish
runners.spotlight: 16-race maiden who is much better known as a sprinter and failed to convince stamina-wise when tried over 7f last month; has even further to go today; too risky.
runners.comment: Much better known as a sprinter; failed to convince stamina-wise over 7f last month

runners.name: Family Fortune
runners.spotlight: Just a respectable fifth on dirt last month, but now only 3lb higher than when winning cosily over C&D for Joao Moreira in December and "Magic Man" is back on board today; must be considered.
runners.comment: Now only 3lb higher than when winning cosily under Joao Moreira over C&D in December
runners.owner: Tony Stradmoor, Billy Li Sze Kuen Et Al

runners.name: Double Bingo
runners.spotlight: Won over 6f here for Me Tsui in November and kept on for fourth over that trip on dirt for new stable a fortnight ago; untested beyond 7f but has often shaped as though this new trip might suit; not ruled out.
runners.comment: Campaigned mainly over 6f but looks worth a crack at this longer trip
runners.owner: Charmaine Wong Kam Mei & Chan Fu Ho

runners.name: Zetta Force
runners.spotlight: 11-race maiden; looked quite promising for another stable last season but has become disappointing this term and is unproven over this far.
runners.comment: Has become disappointing this season and has stamina to prove today

runners.name: Lucky Blessing
runners.spotlight: Reserve; held on gamely to make all over C&D in November but his subsequent form has been underwhelming and he's still 2lb above that winning mark.
runners.comment: Game C&D winner in November but subsequent form has been underwhelming

runners.name: Floof
runners.spotlight: Well down the field on all four starts (1m-1m1f) for new stable since December; nicely drawn today and will appreciate this ease in grade but needs to turn a corner.
runners.comment: Well down the field on all four starts for new stable since December; eased in grade

runners.name: Inno Century
runners.spotlight: Well handicapped on some of last season's 6f form and his last two runs over that trip were respectable, but this first attempt at 1m comes after a three-month break; others have more pressing claims.
runners.comment: In fair form over 6f when last season; this first 1m attempt comes after a break
runners.owner: Tsui Wo Pong, Dennis Lo Yuk Ming Et Al

races.race_name: Winning Try Handicap (Course A) (Turf)

runners.name: Frantanck
runners.spotlight: Arrested decline with respectable fifth over C&D three weeks ago, but has dropped a long way down the weights over the last couple of years and it's hard to tell whether he'll build on that recent effort.
runners.comment: Arrested decline when fifth here last month but does not have an appealing profile

runners.name: Lakeshore Hero
runners.spotlight: Now only 1lb higher than when finishing well for C&D win on seasonal debut in November, but has not been at the same level lately and is drawn on the outside today.
runners.comment: C&D winner in November but below that form lately and has outside draw today

runners.name: Kolachi
runners.spotlight: Kept on for respectable fourth over C&D three weeks ago, but is not quite so well drawn today and his Hong Kong strike-rate now stands at 0-14; needs to find something extra.
runners.comment: Sound effort when fourth over C&D last month but now 0-15 in Hong Kong

runners.name: Good Luck Win
runners.spotlight: Reserve; regressive 6yo who has gone over a year since his last win and is now eligible for lower-grade races; others preferred.
runners.comment: Has gone over a year since his last win and is now eligible for lower-grade races
runners.owner: Siu Wai Chung & Lo Nga Yin

runners.name: Viva Firecracker
runners.spotlight: Seven-race maiden; has shown significant ability over 6f but was a shade disappointing over 7f last month and is not easy to predict over this new trip.
runners.comment: Has shown promise over 6f; safely held over 7f last time; tries a new trip today

runners.name: Win Ease
runners.spotlight: Took step forward when fifth over C&D in February, but well beaten on next two outings (7f/1m) and has now been unplaced in all seven Hong Kong runs.
runners.comment: Well beaten on last two appearances and now 0-7 in Hong Kong

runners.name: The Azure
runners.spotlight: Triple C&D winner last season; back on a very tempting mark now but has not been firing on all cylinders this term and his awkward draw is another negative.
runners.comment: Not firing on all cylinders this season and awkward draw another negative
runners.owner: Mr & Mrs Alan Ho Pui Lun & Mr & Mrs Norman Tang Fai Ng

runners.name: To Infinity
runners.spotlight: Gained all seven wins over C&D; placed on only one of his five starts in 2026, but has endured a poor run of luck on the draw front; back on a workable mark now and, from stall 4, he can't be ruled out.
runners.comment: Seven-time C&D winner; back on a workable mark now and can't be ruled out
runners.owner: Chan Wing Chiu & Yildiz Choi Yat Fan

runners.name: Run Run Timing
runners.spotlight: Resurgent 5yo who made all in Class 5 C&D contest in February and gave it a good shot when second in this higher grade last month; respected in current mood.
runners.comment: Class 5 C&D winner in February and ran well in defeat in this higher grade last month
runners.owner: Tang Ming, Tang Chak Leung Et Al

runners.name: Red Brick Warrior
runners.spotlight: Low-mileage 4yo who looked suited by the step up in trip and made significant ground from the rear when fourth over C&D last month; has inside draw today; no surprise if he takes another step forward.
runners.comment: Low-mileage 4yo; kept on well for fourth over C&D when upped in trip last month

runners.name: Take Action
runners.spotlight: Runner-up here on three of his last four appearances (1m-1m1f), losing out only narrowly on two occasions; remains on a good mark and ought to make his presence felt again.
runners.comment: Runner-up here on three of his last four appearances and remains on a good mark

runners.name: Shooting To Top
runners.spotlight: Reserve; displayed clear promise on first two starts, more recently when keeping-on sixth over C&D last month, but recent 2lb drop is not enough to tempt.
runners.comment: First two runs were quite encouraging but recent 2lb drop is not enough to tempt

runners.name: Fantastic Fun
runners.spotlight: Won twice over C&D in the autumn and looked back in prime form when close second here last month; still on workable mark after recent 2lb nudge and, from stall 3, he's a likely contender again.
runners.comment: Dual C&D winner this season; still on workable mark after last month's close second
runners.owner: Mr & Mrs Hamen Fan Shi Hoo, A Fan Chen Yen & C Fan Chen Mun

runners.name: State Security
runners.spotlight: Made low-key debut over 7f at Sha Tin last month and is probably one for further down the line.
runners.comment: Made low-key debut over 7f last month and is probably one for further down the line
runners.owner: Marc Chan & Lily Chan Li Lei

races.race_name: Successful Conversion Handicap (Course A) (Turf)

runners.name: Romantic Fantasy
runners.spotlight: Runner-up on all four appearances this year, losing out only by a nose over 1m3f here on latest outing three weeks ago; will be fine over this shorter trip but moves up from Class 5 and has a bit more on his plate this time.
runners.comment: Runner-up all four starts this year (1m1f-1m3f) but moves up in grade today

runners.name: Sharpen Bright
runners.spotlight: Hinted at possible revival when fifth here (1m3f) last month but came last over C&D a fortnight later and now has it all to prove again.
runners.comment: Hinted at possible return to form two starts ago but finished last on latest outing

runners.name: Can't Go Wong
runners.spotlight: Kept on for fifth over C&D last month and remains well handicapped, but this season's record is rather mixed and he has a modest strike-rate in Hong Kong (2-49).
runners.comment: Ran okay here last month and remains well handicapped but has a modest strike-rate

runners.name: Romantic Laos
runners.spotlight: Not the force of old but appreciated ease in grade when placed over 1m here last month and this move back up in trip will suit; shortlisted.
runners.comment: Not the force of old but was placed in this grade over 1m last month; shortlisted

runners.name: Smart City
runners.spotlight: Just 1-25 in Hong Kong but has made the frame here on his last four appearances, most recently when placed over C&D last month; ought to run his race again.
